DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

The transaction involved the Development Bank of Wales and Avantis Group's founders, Chris David and Tom David. Avantis Group's existing management team will retain operational control of the business. Blake Morgan acted as legal counsel to the Development Bank of Wales, while Acuity Law advised the founders. AMA Capital Partners acted as corporate finance adviser to Avantis Group.

Reed Smith advised Leon Capital on all aspects of the transaction, providing comprehensive M&A support in connection with the strategic investment. The matter involved a multi-disciplinary team spanning M&A, tax, and regulatory considerations.

The investment supports Avantis Group's long-term growth strategy across maritime and energy markets, including expansion into digital infrastructure and defence, and reflects Leon Capital's confidence in the company's differentiated engineering platform and experienced management team.

For Reed Smith, the transaction represents a further example of the firm's work advising private capital sponsors on complex M&A transactions and continues to strengthen its relationship with Leon Capital as it expands its platform.

The Reed Smith team advising on the matter was led by Andrew Houghton, with support from Ciaran Sweeney, Georgia Moorhouse and Selina Cook on M&A matters; Caspar Fox and Tom Baxter on tax; and Emma Weeden on FDI and antitrust.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH STRENGTHENS ASIA-PACIFIC FINANCE AND TRANSPORTATION CAPABILITY WITH HIRE IN SINGAPORE

SINGAPORE - Global law firm Reed Smith announced the appointment of banking and finance partner Dr. Gregory Xu to its Singapore office. Xu, who joins from Stephenson Harwood, where he led the general banking and project finance offering in Southeast Asia and served on the firm's Supervisory Council, its executive committee, will join Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group.

With over 15 years of experience, Xu advises on high-value, cross-border transactions across a wide range of sectors, including transportation - shipping, aviation, and logistics - trade, life sciences, energy, and technology. He regularly acts for financial institutions, funds, export credit agencies, leasing houses, asset owners, and Fortune 500 companies on complex financing, restructuring, and enforcement matters.

Xu has a strong track record in green and sustainability-linked lending and advises on multi-jurisdictional insolvency-related enforcement and restructuring matters. He also brings extensive experience in general corporate and commercial matters.

Richard Hakes, chair of Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group, said: "Gregory is an outstanding addition to our team, bringing deep knowledge of ship finance, aviation finance, and banking throughout Southeast Asia. His arrival further strengthens our capabilities in Singapore and reinforces our commitment to the maritime, aviation, and broader transportation sectors across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond. Working closely with our shipping teams in Hong Kong and Shanghai and our aviation team in Singapore as part of our global Transportation Industry Group, Gregory will enhance our ability to provide seamless, cross-border support to clients operating in one of the world's most dynamic industries."

Xu commented: "Reed Smith's global platform, sector focus, and collaborative culture are hugely impressive. The firm's market-leading finance practice and its long-standing reputation in general banking, asset finance, and project finance were major draws for me. I'm delighted to be joining such a highly collegiate team with a clear strategy for growth in the region and look forward to contributing to the continued expansion of the firm's finance offering across Asia-Pacific."

Praj Samant, Reed Smith's Asia-Pacific managing partner, said: "We are delighted to welcome Gregory to the Singapore team. His arrival further strengthens our offering in one of the region's most strategically important sectors. We look forward to the valuable contributions he will bring to the firm locally and globally."

Xu is recognized as a leading practitioner by multiple legal directories. Chambers Asia-Pacific ranks him as a "Leading Individual" for Banking & Finance (India), while The Legal 500 identifies him as a "Leading Partner," "Next Generation Partner," and "Recommended" lawyer across Banking & Finance, Asset Finance, Projects, and Shipping in Singapore and in the foreign juris

urn:contentItem:6JH6-GB63-S0HJ-C04B-00000-00PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - RECENT SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:39:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP - RECENT SECTOR HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP

PermID4296383614Websitewww.pillsburylaw.com/en/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress31 West 52nd Street, New York, NY 10019 USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP, founded in 1868, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 700 lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in energy, technology, and finance, specializing in M&A, regulatory, and litigation. Generating over $700 million annually, Pillsbury advised on 2024 renewable energy transactions, recognized by Columbia Law School. Its culture emphasizes innovation, diversity, and pro bono work, with strong associate training programs. Competing with Baker McKenzie, Pillsbury stands out for its energy and tech regulatory expertise. In 2024, it expanded its AI and ESG practices, focusing on sustainable finance. Recent initiatives include cloud-based legal tech and partnerships with nonprofits like Lawyers Alliance for New York, reinforcing its community impact.

+ PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ PILLSBURY OPENS IN BOSTON WITH A FOCUS ON TECHNOLOGY, LIFE SCIENCES AND FINANCIAL INDUSTRIES

Pillsbury is opening a new office in Boston, marking an important expansion into one of the nation's leading growth and innovation centers. The office will launch with a team of five senior attorneys and additional associates, who bring diverse transactional and litigation backgrounds.

Source: Company Website

+ PILLSBURYS MARQUEE INSURANCE RECOVERY & ADVISORY PRACTICE CONTINUES EXPANSION, WELCOMING RENOWNED POLICYHOLDER LITIGATOR BOB GILBERT

Robert J. Gilbert, a nationally recognized litigator who represents policyholders in complex insurance recovery matters, has joined Pillsbury from Latham & Watkins. He comes to the firm as one of the founding partners of Pillsbury's newly established Boston office.

Source: Company Website

+ AS NOVEL DIGITAL RIGHTS ISSUES COME TO THE FORE, PILLSBURY ADDS IP LITIGATOR ROBERT L. URIARTE IN LOS ANGELES

Pillsbury has further enhanced its expansive tech-focused capabilities with the addition of Intellectual Property litigator and trial lawyer Robert L. Uriarte as a partner in its Los Angeles office.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PERKINS COIE [3 Press Releases]

+ PERKINS COIE REPRESENTS SEDRON IN INVESTMENT FROM ARA PARTNERS OF UP TO $500 MILLION

PORTLAND, OR (April 15, 2026)-Perkins Coie announced today that it represented Sedron Technologies, a leader in advanced upcycling systems that transform waste streams into reusable water and fertilizer products, in an investment of up to $500 million from Ara Partners, a global private equity and infrastructure firm focused on industrial decarbonization.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE STRENGTHENS PACIFIC NORTHWEST TRIAL BENCH WITH COMMERCIAL LITIGATOR CHAD COLTON

PORTLAND, OR and SEATTLE, WA (April 6, 2026)-Perkins Coie is pleased to announce that Chad Colton has joined its Commercial Litigation practice as a partner in Portland and Seattle, further strengthening the firm's trial capabilities in the Pacific Northwest.

Bringing two decades of experience, Chad represents clients in high-stakes commercial matters such as partnership, shareholder, breach of contract, IP, and licensing disputes. He also frequently serves as lead trial counsel for bet-the-company cases, often joining to advise and advocate for clients in the middle of litigation or immediately before trial.

Source: Company Website

+ PERKINS COIE RANKS 44TH ON FORTUNE'S "100 BEST COMPANIES TO WORK FOR®" LIST FOR 2026

SEATTLE (April 1, 2026)-Perkins Coie is proud to announce that it has again been named to Fortune's "100 Best Companies to Work For®" list for 2026, marking the 24th consecutive year that the firm has been featured on Fortune's prestigious list.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COOLEY LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ COOLEY LLP VETHOS ANNOUNCES $200 MILLION IPO

Cooley advised Ethos Technologies, a top life insurance technology company on a mission to democratize access to life insurance, on its $200 million initial public offering (IPO). Ethos offered 5,127,696 shares of Class A common stock, and selling stockholders offered an additional 5,398,619 shares of Class A common stock, at a price of $19 per share. Ethos began trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on January 29, 2026, under the ticker symbol LIFE.

Partner Matthew Bartus led the Cooley team advising Ethos since its formation in 2016, including on its $100 million Series D-1 in July 2021 and $200 million Series D in May 2021.

Lawyers Jon Avina, Jean Park, Milson Yu, Samara Zaifman, Katie Lapidus and Charlotte Yin led the Cooley team advising Ethos on its IPO.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP INFERACT ANNOUNCES $150 MILLION SEED FINANCING

Cooley advised Inferact, a startup founded by creators and core maintainers of the open-source project vLLM, on its $150 million seed financing at an $800 million valuation. The financing was co-led by Andreessen Horowitz and Lightspeed Venture Partners, with investments from Sequoia Capital, Altimeter Capital, Redpoint Ventures, ZhenFund, The House Fund, Striker Venture Partners, Laude Ventures, Databricks Ventures and the UC Berkeley Chancellor's Fund.

Lawyers Peter Werner, Tara Capsuto, Won Lee, Carl Min and Isabella Ysebrands led the Cooley team advising Inferact.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP OPENEVIDENCE RAISES $250 MILLION SERIES D

Cooley advised OpenEvidence, the most widely used artificial intelligence (AI) platform by doctors in America, on its $250 million Series D financing, valuing the company at $12 billion and making it the most valuable healthcare AI company in the world. The new funding will be used to invest heavily in the research and development and compute costs associated with the multi-AI agentic architecture of OpenEvidence, which provides the highest quality and most accurate medical answers of any system in the world.

The round was co-led by Thrive Capital and DST Global, and brings the total funding raised by OpenEvidence to nearly $700 million over the past year.

Lawyers Derek Colla, David Safren and Sela Dougherty led the Cooley team advising OpenEvidence.

Source: Company Website

+ COOLEY LLP NEUROPHOS SECURES $110 MILLION SERIES A

Cooley advised Neurophos, a leader in photonic artificial intelligence chip technology, on its oversubscribed $110 million Series A round, bringing total funding to $118 million. The new funding will accelerate the delivery of Neurophos' first integrated photonic compute system, including datacenter-ready optical processing unit modules, a full software stack and early access developer hardware.

The round was led by Gates Frontier, with participation from M12 (Microsoft's venture fund), Carbon Direct Capital, Aramco Ventures, Bosch Ventures, Tectonic Ventures, Space Capital and others.

Lawyers James Allen, Nick Hobson, James Reizman and Nathan Alamillo led the Cooley team advising Neurophos.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ K&L GATES [3 Press Releases]

+ TWO K&L GATES PARTNERS AGAIN NAMED TO INDIA BUSINESS LAW JOURNALS INTERNATIONAL A-LIST 2026

K&L Gates is pleased to announce that Singapore Office Managing Partner Raja Bose and Singapore Corporate partner Meraj Noor have once again been named to India Business Law Journal's (IBLJ) International A-List. This marks the eighth consecutive year both partners have received this recognition.

IBLJ's International A-List identifies the top lawyers based outside of India who are recognized for their work on India-related matters. The A-List was compiled by IBLJ's seasoned editorial team following extensive market research, drawing on feedback from hundreds of lawyers at Indian law firms and thousands of India-focused in-house counsel within India and abroad.

The full International A-List 2026 is featured on IBLJ's website.

**All Singapore lawyers are members of K&L Gates Straits Law LLC, the Singapore office of global law firm K&L Gates. Source: Company Website

+ MORE THAN 80 K&L GATES LAWYERS ACROSS ASIA-PACIFIC RECOGNISED IN 2027 BEST LAWYERS, ONES TO WATCH EDITIONS

Best Lawyers®, a peer-review publication in the legal profession, has named more than 80 K&L Gates lawyers across the Asia-Pacific region in the 2027 editions of its guides, covering Australia, Japan, and Singapore. In addition, nearly 20 of the firm's lawyers were named as Best Lawyers: Ones to Watch® in Australia and Japan, lists that recognise lawyers early in their careers.

Alongside these recognitions, four Australian partners were recognised with "Lawyer of the Year" awards, an accolade that is particularly noteworthy, as it is only bestowed on one standout lawyer for each practice area in each jurisdiction.

The Best Lawyers® guides highlight top legal talent, selected by peers within the same geographical region and legal practice area, through millions of votes each year. Congratulations to the Lawyer of the Year award recipients below, as well as all lawyers recognised in the 2027 editions - resulting in nearly 180 total recognitions for the firm. Source: Company Website

+ K&L GATES ADVISES DOLPHIN ENTERTAINMENT, INC. ON US$15 MILLION PIPE INVESTMENT

Global law firm K&L Gates LLP advised Dolphin Entertainment, Inc., an entertainment marketing and premium content production company, in connection with a US$15 million private investment in public equity (PIPE) financing with Lincoln Park Capital, an investment group and asset management firm.

The Corporate team advising on the transaction was led by Miami partner Clayton Parker with support from Miami partner Erin Fogarty.

"This transaction underscores Dolphin Entertainment's commitment to accelerating strategic growth across its marketing and content-driven businesses. We were pleased to support them in securing investment from a partner aligned with its long-term vision," said Parker.

K&L Gates' Corporate practice is one of the most substantial in the legal industry, with hundreds of lawyers in offices across the globe providing clients with practical legal solutions in the structuring, financing, and completion of domestic, international, and cross-border transactions. Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ REED SMITH LLP [4 Press Releases]

+ REED SMITH ADVISES NATURPAK ON SALE BY TRIVEST TO PPC

CHICAGO - Global law firm Reed Smith represented Trivest Partners and IPMF, LLC, doing business as NaturPak, a manufacturer of bone broths, soups, sauces, wet pet food, and food ingredients for national retailers and brands, on its sale by Trivest Growth Investment Fund II to Pritzker Private Capital ("PPC"). Trivest Growth Investment Fund II, managed by Trivest Partners LP, a private equity firm focused exclusively on investments in founder- and family owned businesses, partnered with NaturPak through a minority investment in 2020. During Trivest's partnership, NaturPak transformed from a small specialty co-manufacturer into a scaled, multi-segment platform, increasing its R&D and manufacturing capabilities and production capacity.

The cross-office Reed Smith deal team included attorneys in Chicago, New York, Denver, Philadelphia, Dallas, Orange County (California), Pittsburgh, Washington, Houston, Tysons (Virginia), and Miami. The team was led by Nick Gibson, Kristen Capriotti, Crockett Stevenson, and Sudeep Kalkunte, with support from Constantine Karides, Brad Schmarak, David Hayes, Simon Kliegman, Ian Sherwin, Paulo McKeeby, Daniel Hagedorn, Michelle Mantine, Cori Goldberg, A.J. Wissinger, Jillian Burstein, Tyler Thompson, Mike Summers, Michael Jacobs, Brendan Leanos, Benjamin Brimeyer, Michael Galibois, Michael Lowell, Joseph Mammone Jr., Michelle DiPuma, Batool Banker, Leah Hungerman, Taber Rueter, Clirae Bourke, Michaela Mapes, Peter Loy, Talia Boiangin Behar, Elliott Race, George Avdellas, Katherine Litaker, Taylor Kollmansberger, and Nikita Bazaj.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH PARTNERS JOSH LOVE, ERIC MARDER, STEPHEN SESSA, AND EDWARD SHAPIRO NAMED TO VARIETY'S "DEALMAKERS 2025" REPORT

LOS ANGELES - Global law firm Reed Smith announced today that Josh Love, Eric Marder, Stephen Sessa, and Edward Shapiro, partners in the firm's global Entertainment & Media Industry Group, have been named to Variety's "Dealmakers 2025" report. Love, Marder, Sessa, and Shapiro are among an elite group of entertainment attorneys who have been chosen based on their more than $3 billion in music transactions in 2025, high‑profile client roster, and leadership across the music industry. The publication highlights their work on "sports and music transactions, start-ups and brands," "recording, music publishing, and live performance agreements (including Bad Bunny's upcoming Super Bowl halftime show)," and "catalog acquisitions."

This recognition reflects Reed Smith's continued leadership in high-stakes entertainment and music transactions, talent representation, and emerging legal issues at the intersection of entertainment, music, technology, and finance. The four partners were selected for their high-profile talent negotiations and representation business.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH ADVISES LEON CAPITAL ON STRATEGIC INVESTMENT IN AVANTIS GROUP

LONDON - Global law firm Reed Smith has advised funds advised by Leon Capital on the strategic investment in Avantis Group, a UK-based provider of specialist engineering services to the maritime and energy industries. The transaction, which represents the latest new investment by Leon Capital, completed on 23 December 2025. The terms of the investment are confidential.

The transaction involved the Development Bank of Wales and Avantis Group's founders, Chris David and Tom David. Avantis Group's existing management team will retain operational control of the business. Blake Morgan acted as legal counsel to the Development Bank of Wales, while Acuity Law advised the founders. AMA Capital Partners acted as corporate finance adviser to Avantis Group.

Reed Smith advised Leon Capital on all aspects of the transaction, providing comprehensive M&A support in connection with the strategic investment. The matter involved a multi-disciplinary team spanning M&A, tax, and regulatory considerations.

The investment supports Avantis Group's long-term growth strategy across maritime and energy markets, including expansion into digital infrastructure and defence, and reflects Leon Capital's confidence in the company's differentiated engineering platform and experienced management team.

For Reed Smith, the transaction represents a further example of the firm's work advising private capital sponsors on complex M&A transactions and continues to strengthen its relationship with Leon Capital as it expands its platform.

The Reed Smith team advising on the matter was led by Andrew Houghton, with support from Ciaran Sweeney, Georgia Moorhouse and Selina Cook on M&A matters; Caspar Fox and Tom Baxter on tax; and Emma Weeden on FDI and antitrust.

Source: Company Website

+ REED SMITH STRENGTHENS ASIA-PACIFIC FINANCE AND TRANSPORTATION CAPABILITY WITH HIRE IN SINGAPORE

SINGAPORE - Global law firm Reed Smith announced the appointment of banking and finance partner Dr. Gregory Xu to its Singapore office. Xu, who joins from Stephenson Harwood, where he led the general banking and project finance offering in Southeast Asia and served on the firm's Supervisory Council, its executive committee, will join Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group.

With over 15 years of experience, Xu advises on high-value, cross-border transactions across a wide range of sectors, including transportation - shipping, aviation, and logistics - trade, life sciences, energy, and technology. He regularly acts for financial institutions, funds, export credit agencies, leasing houses, asset owners, and Fortune 500 companies on complex financing, restructuring, and enforcement matters.

Xu has a strong track record in green and sustainability-linked lending and advises on multi-jurisdictional insolvency-related enforcement and restructuring matters. He also brings extensive experience in general corporate and commercial matters.

Richard Hakes, chair of Reed Smith's Transportation Industry Group, said: "Gregory is an outstanding addition to our team, bringing deep knowledge of ship finance, aviation finance, and banking throughout Southeast Asia. His arrival further strengthens our capabilities in Singapore and reinforces our commitment to the maritime, aviation, and broader transportation sectors across the Asia-Pacific region and beyond. Working closely with our shipping teams in Hong Kong and Shanghai and our aviation team in Singapore as part of our global Transportation Industry Group, Gregory will enhance our ability to provide seamless, cross-border support to clients operating in one of the world's most dynamic industries."

Xu commented: "Reed Smith's global platform, sector focus, and collaborative culture are hugely impressive. The firm's market-leading finance practice and its long-standing reputation in general banking, asset finance, and project finance were major draws for me. I'm delighted to be joining such a highly collegiate team with a clear strategy for growth in the region and look forward to contributing to the continued expansion of the firm's finance offering across Asia-Pacific."

Praj Samant, Reed Smith's Asia-Pacific managing partner, said: "We are delighted to welcome Gregory to the Singapore team. His arrival further strengthens our offering in one of the region's most strategically important sectors. We look forward to the valuable contributions he will bring to the firm locally and globally."

Xu is recognized as a leading practitioner by multiple legal directories. Chambers Asia-Pacific ranks him as a "Leading Individual" for Banking & Finance (India), while The Legal 500 identifies him as a "Leading Partner," "Next Generation Partner," and "Recommended" lawyer across Banking & Finance, Asset Finance, Projects, and Shipping in Singapore and in the foreign juris

value.Title: California investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind project
value.Overview: The Record (Troy, New York), NATIONAL POLITICS, 599words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH9-GYY3-S9NN-T0W8-00000-00California investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind project2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:39:34ZLexisNexisNATIONAL POLITICSCalifornia investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind projectJENNIFER McDERMOTT

California is investigating one of the Trump administration's deals to end an offshore wind project.

Golden State Wind was a floating offshore wind project proposed off California's central coast. The California Energy Commission said Monday it issued an administrative subpoena to Golden State Wind.

The commission said it is seeking documents and information about the company's recent agreement with the Department of Interior to accept a payout in exchange for voluntarily abandoning its offshore wind lease.

"The Trump administration is recklessly spending billions of taxpayer dollars on backroom deals that would turn back the clock on innovation," CEC Chair David Hochschild said in a statement. "Californians deserve immediate answers about the nature of this payout. Taxpayer dollars should be used to build a sustainable energy future, not to pay to make projects disappear."

The Trump administration is spending nearly $2 billion to get energy companies to walk away from U.S. offshore wind projects. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum said companies were sold a product that was only viable when propped up by massive taxpayer subsidies when they bid for these offshore wind leases in 2022, under former President Joe Biden.

The Republican administration adopted this strategy after federal courts thwarted President Donald Trump's efforts to stop offshore wind development through executive action. Three agreements have been announced.

Under the first deal, made public in March, French company TotalEnergies is getting $1 billion essentially a refund of its leases for offshore wind projects off North Carolina and New York if it invests the money in fossil fuel projects instead. In the latest deals announced last week, the administration said Golden State Wind and Bluepoint Wind agreed to end their leases in exchange for reimbursements totaling nearly $900 million, provided they invest equally in fossil fuels.

Both Golden State and Bluepoint are co-owned by Ocean Winds, a joint venture of EDP Renewables and French energy giant Engie. Bluepoint Wind was an offshore wind project in the early stages of development off the coasts of New Jersey and New York.

When asked about the subpoena Monday, Ocean Winds said it does not comment on open or potential litigation.

This investigation sets the stage for legal action from California to safeguard renewable energy, as well as the thousands of jobs and millions of dollars of investment the state was counting on, said Eddie Ahn, executive director of Brightline Defense, an environmental justice nonprofit working to advance offshore wind in California.

A letter from California Attorney General Rob Bonta's office to Golden State Wind says the state anticipates potential litigation involving the federal government and parties to lease buyouts impacting California's energy needs and offshore wind programs. California has invested about $100 million to support offshore wind development in order to accelerate the state's transition to clean energy and address climate change.

Democrats in Congress are investigating, too. U.S. Reps. Jared Huffman of California, the top Democrat on the House Natural Resources Committee, and Jamie Raskin, the ranking Democrat on the House Judiciary Committee, are demanding information about TotalEnergies agreement.

The Associated Press' climate and environmental coverage receives financial support from multiple private foundations. AP is solely responsible for all content. Find AP's standards for working with philanthropies, a list of supporters and funded coverage areas at AP.org.

value.Title: MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - INDUSTRY IN FOCUS: TRENDS & PEER ACTIVITY

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHB-9XG3-RSRS-S52C-00000-00MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - INDUSTRY IN FOCUS: TRENDS & PEER ACTIVITY2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:39:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKMCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - INDUSTRY IN FOCUS: TRENDS & PEER ACTIVITY

DASHBOARD: MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC.

PermID4296686586Websitehttp://www.mccarthy.com/IndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress1604 Eastport Plaza Drive, Suite 106 (Map it) Collinsville, Illinois 62234

ACTIVITIES:

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc., founded in 1864, is an employee-owned construction firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 7,000 employees, it provides preconstruction and construction services for healthcare, commercial, and renewable energy projects, generating $5.5 billion in 2023 revenue. Notable projects include the Mayo Clinic expansion. McCarthys focus on safety, quality, and technology, including BIM, ensures efficient project delivery. Serving clients like Kaiser Permanente, it competes with Skanska while fostering a collaborative culture and sustainability initiatives, maintaining a strong presence in the U.S. construction market.

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. [4 Press Releases]

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES TOPS OUT GILEAD SCIENCES' NEW TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT CENTER IN FOSTER CITY

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. (McCarthy), recently celebrated the topping out of the New Technical Development Center (NTDC) at Gilead Sciences' headquarters in Foster City, marking a major construction milestone with the completion of the building's structural frame. The work is in collaboration with design partners DGA Architects, Salas O'Brien Structural Engineers and civil designer BKF Engineer.

Project partners, field teams and leaders gathered on site to celebrate the center's topping out, marking placement of the final beam and the structure reaching full height. The centuries-old tradition honors the collective effort behind the milestone and signals that structural work is nearing completion as the project moves into its next phase.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: EXPANDING HOSPITAL PARKING STRUCTURES FOR BETTER CARE

Driving your child to the hospital is already a harrowing experience. Finding parking once you arrive should not be your biggest concern. When parking structures prioritize flexibility, enhance connectivity, and respond to evolving patient and staff needs, they make hospital visits a little less daunting.

In early 2025, as McCarthy Building Companies completed the Southwest Tower at Children's Hospital of Orange County (CHOC), in Orange, California, the construction firm also expanded the facility's parking structure. This project involved expanding the structure from 522,000 to 912,000 square feet by adding three vertical levels as well as another horizontal bay to connect the new outpatient tower and the existing main campus. However, the expansion was about more than connecting buildings. It increased the efficiency and sustainability of the overall structure by accommodating a growing number of patients and staff within this care-centric environment.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: BRIGHT FUTURE: A'JEYA ANTHONY'S PATH FROM NSBE TO MCCARTHY

Our goal to be the best builder in America requires an inclusive talent acquisition strategy that enables us to find and recruit the best talent. By expanding our reach through organizations like the National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE), we can connect with emerging leaders and create more pathways into our industry.

A'Jeya Anthony is a three-time project intern at McCarthy Building Companies. Her journey is just one example of how meeting top candidates where they are help us to connect with exceptional people who strengthen our workforce and business.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. RYAN HOUSE DEBUTS TEEN SUITES FOR KIDS LIVING LONGER WITH LIFE-LIMITING CONDITIONS

Ryan House, a nonprofit that provides respite and end-of-life care to children with life-limiting conditions, recently capped off its 15th anniversary year by unveiling two newly renovated teen suites. The renovations allow Ryan House to better serve children who are now surviving well into adolescence and young adulthood and were made possible through generous support from McCarthy Building Companies, which selected Ryan House as a recipient of the company's Heart Hats(TradeMark) grants, and ARC Document Solutions.

Thanks to advances in diagnostics, disease management, and comprehensive care, many children with life-limiting conditions are now living longer than once expected. Ryan House is evolving alongside the children and families it serves, making meaningful changes to better support teens through environments designed specifically for this stage of life.

The renovation was spearheaded by McCarthy's Specialized Solutions Group, which focuses on complex renovations and technical builds within healthcare and other specialized environments. The donation involved the renovation of two private suites specifically designed for teenagers and features superhero-themed decor provided by ARC that fully immerses them in a comic book-inspired atmosphere. The rooms also now include fully adjustable desks to accommodate different mobility needs, allowing teens to comfortably do schoolwork or creative activities. Each suite is equipped with the latest state-of-the-art gaming system and adaptive controllers, plus customizable LED lighting that can change colors based on each teen's preferences. Additionally, bathroom updates were made to allow for enhanced comfort and accessibility.

"This renovation speaks to how pediatric care has changed over the past decade and a half," said Dr. Wendy Bernatavicius, medical director at Ryan House and division chief of primary, complex care and adolescent medicine at Phoenix Children's Hospital. "We're seeing more adolescents and young adults with complex needs living vibrant lives. Improved medical care, early intervention and whole-family support have extended the lifespan of many of these teens. That means rethinking the environments where they're cared for. These teen suites are not just rooms. They're an affirmation of identity, belonging, and respect for each young person who calls Ryan House their home away from home."

During their recent holiday break, Ryan House welcomed two familiar guests, brothers Mason and Jaxton, who are living with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, as the first teens to stay in the newly renovated suites. While they've stayed at Ryan House before, this visit marked the first time they experienced the upgraded rooms designed specifically with teens, like them, in mind.

"The gift of these teen suites is the culmination of our vision for this next chapter," said Tracy Leonard Warner, executive director of Ryan House. "This home has been serving families

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [1 Press Release]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION LABOR SHORTAGE LOOMS AS CONSTRUCTION REBOUNDS

SANTA CLARA, Calif., Dec. 9, 2025 - Just as individual market sectors are finding ways to move projects forward after a year of supply chain and cost turbulence, the ongoing labor shortage threatens to bog down construction unless owners and contractors embrace new approaches, according to the latest Market Conditions Report from DPR Construction, one of the nation's top technical builders.

A recent survey by the Associated General Contractors of America and the national Center for Construction Education and Research showed that:

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER PROVIDES AI-POWERED SAFETY TOOL TO THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY

Turner is making SafeT Coach, its AI-powered field safety tool, available free of charge to the broader construction industry. The announcement comes during Turner's 22nd annual Safety Week and company-wide Safety Stand-Down, it's largest to date, under the theme "All In Together."

In construction, conditions change frequently, and the traditional safety resources that establish standards-manuals, scheduled training, retrospective reporting-are not always accessible when and where field teams need them most. SafeT Coach was developed to close that gap.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION HASLAM SPORTS GROUP, AECOM HUNT AND TURNER JV BREAK GROUND ON HUNTINGTON BANK FIELD

Construction of New State-of-the-Art Facility to Serve as Ohio's First Enclosed Stadium Revolutionary Design by HKS to Bring Football Fans Closer to the Field Than EverThe Haslam Sports Group (HSG) alongside a joint venture of AECOM Hunt and Turner Construction Company and HKS today announced the groundbreaking of the new Huntington Bank Field, the future home of the Cleveland Browns starting in the 2029 season. The groundbreaking ceremony held at the new site in Brook Park today marks the official start of construction on Northeast Ohio's largest economic development project to date, the new Huntington Bank Field enclosed stadium and surrounding district.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION WHERE SMALL CHANGES CREATE REAL IMPACT

Turner's support to the Paraplegic Foundation reflects a focus on improving everyday spaces at Sanjeevandeep Special School. The initiative has contributed to creating a more accessible, comfortable environment for children and caregivers.Our recent association with the Paraplegic Foundation came from a shared belief that everyone deserves the opportunity to live with dignity, comfort, and as much independence as possible. Through their work, the Foundation supports initiatives like Sanjeevandeep Special School, a space dedicated to children with special needs, where care, learning, and rehabilitation come together in a very real, hands-on way.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [2 Press Releases]

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION BREAKS GROUND ON $190 MILLION CONTRACT FOR STATE-OF-THE-ART DRINKING WATER FACILITY

DENVER, Colo. (Sept. 25, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. celebrated the groundbreaking of a $190 million contract to build the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility - an innovative project that employs advanced treatment technology to ensure clean water to residents.

The City of Westminster entrusted the PCL team to build the project's most critical components. As the lead contractor, PCL will manage the construction of cutting-edge filtration and treatment technologies. The facility will feature advanced oxidation and filtration systems designed to improve taste and odor, while enhancing resiliency against fluctuations in incoming water quality.

Designed with long-term sustainability in mind, the project replaces aging infrastructure and will provide reliable drinking water to nearly 33,000 homes and businesses. Once operational the facility will have the capacity to treat 14.7 million gallons of water per day (MGD).

"PCL is proud to celebrate the opening of the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility with the City of Westminster. This is an essential project that secures safe, reliable drinking water for the community," said Ankur Talwar, PCL's West region district manager.

The groundbreaking ceremony brought together Westminster City Council members, build partners, local leaders, and community members to celebrate the future site.

The facility is slated to be finished in 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION AND PHOENIX CHILDREN'S: BUILDING A STRONGER FUTURE FOR PEDIATRIC CARE

PHOENIX, Ariz. (November 10, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. and Phoenix Children's are proud to celebrate a partnership dedicated to advancing pediatric health and supporting children facing critical medical challenges in Arizona.

For eight consecutive years, PCL Construction in Arizona has deepened its commitment to Phoenix Children's, increasing its annual donation to $50,000 in support of pediatric cancer care. This ongoing collaboration directly benefits children by funding medical specialists, cutting-edge technology, and family-centered care.

"Our partnership with Phoenix Children's is about more than sponsorship-it's about building hope and supporting families during their most difficult times," said Josh Yates, construction risk manager at PCL Construction. "We are honored to contribute to the hospital's mission and to help create brighter futures for children in our community."

As part of this partnership, PCL proudly sponsors Phoenix Children's annual Big Dig for Kids event. This unique experience gives pediatric patients a chance to explore construction equipment, participate in hands-on activities, and enjoy a day filled with fun and inspiration. The event not only raises vital funds for pediatric cancer care but also brings moments of joy to children and families, helping them envision a future beyond illness.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE, LLC [1 Press Release]

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE, LLC MOUNT SINAI MEDICAL CENTER REACHES TOPPING OUT MILESTONE FOR NEW WESTCHESTER EMERGENCY AND SPECIALTY CARE FACILITY

Construction crews have placed the final beam atop Mount Sinai Medical Center's new Westchester Freestanding Emergency Department, marking a major milestone in expanding access to high-quality, around-the-clock care for residents in western Miami-Dade County. The new facility will bring Mount Sinai's trusted emergency and specialty care closer to home for thousands of area families, helping reduce travel times and meet growing healthcare needs.

Located in Westchester, the four-story, 112,000 square-foot facility-designed by HuntonBrady Architects-will feature 26 exam bays, a Baker Act receiving unit, and advanced imaging services such as MRI, CT, and ultrasound. The project also features a detached two-story central energy plant and medical gas building, demolition of two existing structures and six acres of site improvements. Construction is expected to be complete late 2026.

"This milestone is a tribute to the skill and dedication of every crew member who's helped bring this facility to life," said Mike Peters, vice president and division manager at Brasfield & Gorrie. "Partnering with Mount Sinai Medical Center and HuntonBrady Architects on this important project highlights what can be achieved through teamwork and craftsmanship. Our crews take great pride in knowing their work will help make emergency and specialty care more accessible for families across Miami-Dade County."

Source: Company Website

value.Title: California investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind project
value.Overview: Daily Local News (West Chester, Pennsylvania), NATIONAL POLITICS, 599words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH9-GYY3-S9NN-T05C-00000-00California investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind project2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:39:34ZLexisNexisNATIONAL POLITICSCalifornia investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind projectJENNIFER McDERMOTT

California is investigating one of the Trump administration's deals to end an offshore wind project.

Golden State Wind was a floating offshore wind project proposed off California's central coast. The California Energy Commission said Monday it issued an administrative subpoena to Golden State Wind.

The commission said it is seeking documents and information about the company's recent agreement with the Department of Interior to accept a payout in exchange for voluntarily abandoning its offshore wind lease.

"The Trump administration is recklessly spending billions of taxpayer dollars on backroom deals that would turn back the clock on innovation," CEC Chair David Hochschild said in a statement. "Californians deserve immediate answers about the nature of this payout. Taxpayer dollars should be used to build a sustainable energy future, not to pay to make projects disappear."

The Trump administration is spending nearly $2 billion to get energy companies to walk away from U.S. offshore wind projects. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum said companies were sold a product that was only viable when propped up by massive taxpayer subsidies when they bid for these offshore wind leases in 2022, under former President Joe Biden.

The Republican administration adopted this strategy after federal courts thwarted President Donald Trump's efforts to stop offshore wind development through executive action. Three agreements have been announced.

Under the first deal, made public in March, French company TotalEnergies is getting $1 billion essentially a refund of its leases for offshore wind projects off North Carolina and New York if it invests the money in fossil fuel projects instead. In the latest deals announced last week, the administration said Golden State Wind and Bluepoint Wind agreed to end their leases in exchange for reimbursements totaling nearly $900 million, provided they invest equally in fossil fuels.

Both Golden State and Bluepoint are co-owned by Ocean Winds, a joint venture of EDP Renewables and French energy giant Engie. Bluepoint Wind was an offshore wind project in the early stages of development off the coasts of New Jersey and New York.

When asked about the subpoena Monday, Ocean Winds said it does not comment on open or potential litigation.

This investigation sets the stage for legal action from California to safeguard renewable energy, as well as the thousands of jobs and millions of dollars of investment the state was counting on, said Eddie Ahn, executive director of Brightline Defense, an environmental justice nonprofit working to advance offshore wind in California.

A letter from California Attorney General Rob Bonta's office to Golden State Wind says the state anticipates potential litigation involving the federal government and parties to lease buyouts impacting California's energy needs and offshore wind programs. California has invested about $100 million to support offshore wind development in order to accelerate the state's transition to clean energy and address climate change.

Democrats in Congress are investigating, too. U.S. Reps. Jared Huffman of California, the top Democrat on the House Natural Resources Committee, and Jamie Raskin, the ranking Democrat on the House Judiciary Committee, are demanding information about TotalEnergies agreement.

urn:contentItem:6JH4-NVK3-RRT8-V30S-00000-00SWINERTON INCORPORATED - THE RECENT HEADLINES WITH PEER NEWS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:39:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKSWINERTON INCORPORATED - THE RECENT HEADLINES WITH PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: SWINERTON INCORPORATED

PermID4296594671Websitehttp://www.swinerton.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress5707 SOUTHWEST PARKWAY, BLDG. 1, SUITE 200 | AUSTIN, TX 78735

ACTIVITIES:

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. This approach encourages initiative, whether it involves innovating on complex tasks, stepping into new roles, or contributing ideas beyond formal job descriptions. Employees benefit from career paths aligned with their aspirations, supported by various learning and development programs.The research conducted by Great Place To Work® corroborates DPR's employee-centric strategy, revealing that a significant portion of the workforce feels a sense of pride in their contributions and a strong sense of belonging within the company. The certification further enhances DPR's reputation as an employer of choice, with additional accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek recognizing it as one of the best companies to work for, particularly in over 15 U.S. cities over the past year.DPR Construction has built a strong legacy since its founding in 1990, evolving into a multi-billion-dollar organization that specializes in complex and sustainable projects across various sectors. Its commitment to innovation, through the use of virtual design and construction and self-performing craft, positions DPR among the top contractors in the industry, employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide. For more details about DPR's culture and career opportunities, visit their official website.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION MARKS KEY MILESTONE WITH ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER

FREDERICK, MD — Turner Construction Company celebrated the topping out of the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, marking the completion of the structural phase for the 72-megawatt facility. This significant milestone was achieved on the 28th of April 2026, less than five months after breaking ground, showcasing the effectiveness of early planning and strong collaboration among the project team.To date, the project has seen over 80,000 craft hours logged, with approximately 345 skilled workers contributing to its progress. "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame," stated Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner Construction Company. "It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner’s commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently."The Aligned IAD-06 Data Center spans 450,000 square feet and is situated on a 75-acre campus designed to meet the increasing digital infrastructure demands of the region. The facility features a two-story structural steel design, an insulated precast exterior system, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling solution that combines both liquid and air-cooled technologies to optimize operational performance.The successful topping out of the IAD-06 Data Center underscores Turner Construction's expertise in managing complex projects in the data center sector, which is rapidly evolving to accommodate the surge in digital services. The collaborative efforts demonstrated by Turner and its partners are pivotal in ensuring that the project meets its ambitious timeline and quality standards.As the project moves into the next phases of development, stakeholders will be closely monitoring the advancements made by Turner Construction and Aligned. + TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMMENCES VERTICAL BUILD ON $900M GEISINGER EXPANSION

WILKES-BARRE, PA — Turner Construction has begun vertical construction on the $900 million expansion of Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center, marking a major milestone in one of northeastern Pennsylvania’s largest health care investments. The date of this announcement is 28th April 2026.After years of planning and foundational work, steel is now rising on the 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower. This new structure will increase the hospital's capacity from 356 to 500 beds and expand the number of operating rooms from 12 to 18. Additionally, the emergency department will be enlarged, supporting Geisinger’s goal of providing all private patient rooms. The facility is expected to open in the first quarter of 2028.David Kaminski, Vice President & Senior General Manager at Turner Construction, stated, “This milestone marks an important step forward in delivering expanded, modern health care access for families across northeastern Pennsylvania. We are proud to partner with Geisinger on a transformational project that will enhance patient care, support caregivers, and strengthen this community for decades to come.”The project is anticipated to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate approximately $1 billion in local economic impact. The onsite workforce is expected to grow from 150 to as many as 450 workers during peak activity. Upon completion, Geisinger plans to hire an additional 400 employees, further contributing to local job creation.Originally opened in 1981, the Geisinger Wyoming Valley campus has expanded over the decades to meet the growing demand for health services. This latest addition will bring advanced medical capabilities comparable to those found in major academic medical centers located in large metropolitan areas.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION SELECTED FOR AMBITIOUS WESTCOURT ORLANDO PROJECT

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, an ambitious development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone in the project’s progression.The decision to partner with Turner Construction stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures, the project developer. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, noted that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, especially along Florida’s Space Coast, which includes coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches. Chapman recalled an early meeting where he expressed confidence in their ability to tackle the Westcourt project, saying, “If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us.”Turner Construction has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, focusing on pre-construction activities alongside JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Jeff Justen, vice president and general manager of Turner’s Orlando Business Unit, indicated that the early site activities have commenced, including utility mapping and surveying. These preparatory measures are critical for ensuring a smooth transition into the next phases of construction.The Westcourt project has faced some delays, shifting timelines due to financing challenges and fluctuations in construction costs. However, Chapman emphasized that involving Turner Construction early in the process allowed for valuable input on design and execution strategies. “We don’t want to design in a vacuum,” he said, highlighting the importance of contractor collaboration during the planning stages.Valued at approximately $500 million, the Westcourt development aims to create a vibrant mixed-use space featuring a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center. During peak construction, the project is expected to support around 800 workers on-site, contributing to the local job market in Central Florida. Chapman expressed excitement over the project’s complexity, stating that JMA Ventures is drawn to intricate developments, which informed their choice in partners like Turner Construction.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION TACKLES COLORADO RIVER CRISIS WITH INNOVATIVE WATER SOLUTIONS

As the Colorado River faces unprecedented challenges, PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. is stepping up to provide innovative solutions aimed at water resilience. On the 26th of March 2026, the urgency surrounding the Colorado River's dwindling water supply became a focal point for communities across the Western United States, as they seek alternatives to secure their water futures.The Colorado River serves as a vital resource for millions, yet it is currently grappling with severe drought conditions. Lake Powell, for instance, is at an alarming 162 feet below full capacity, holding only 26% of its total water volume. The U.S. Bureau of Reclamation warns that if current conditions persist, Lake Mead could reach record lows within two years, prompting a critical reassessment of water management strategies across the basin states.PCL Construction is at the forefront of this reassessment, advocating for investments in water reuse infrastructure as a key strategy for bolstering water security. With projected U.S. investments in water reuse expected to average $47.1 billion over the next decade, PCL is positioned to support municipalities in capturing and reusing water to mitigate the effects of ongoing drought. Although adoption rates vary significantly among the seven Colorado River basin states, PCL's initiatives exemplify how infrastructure investments can enhance resilience in water supply systems.The urgency of the situation is underscored by the imminent expiration of current water management rules in the fall of 2026. As states grapple with the looming deadline to ratify Colorado River water allocation agreements, PCL Construction's projects, such as the Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix, provide tangible examples of how communities can adapt to a drier future. This program has been crucial in ensuring the reliability of water supply for the rapidly growing Phoenix area, which has seen a 12% population increase over the last decade.Industry leaders, including PCL, recently gathered at the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference to discuss collaborative strategies for securing the river's future. The consensus was clear: a multifaceted approach is necessary to address the complexities of climate change and aging infrastructure. PCL Construction's commitment to advancing water reuse initiatives positions the company as a critical player in shaping sustainable water management practices that can withstand the challenges posed by the Colorado River's ongoing crisis.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ADVANCES $192M AI-READY DATA CENTER PROJECT

PCL Construction Services, Inc. and PCL Industrial Services, Inc., operating companies of PCL Construction, have reached structural completion on a $192 million, 22.5-megawatt data center, advancing one of the largest digital infrastructure projects currently underway in the Denver metropolitan area. On 19th March 2026, the companies celebrated this significant milestone that underscores their commitment to delivering state-of-the-art facilities designed for high-capacity digital workloads.Located on a 17-acre site southeast of Denver, the project is being delivered by PCL’s Minneapolis Buildings group in collaboration with the Denver team, combining longstanding client relationships with local market expertise. The data center is specifically designed to support enterprise-scale, compute-intensive workloads, including artificial intelligence and graphics processing unit applications, as demand for high-capacity digital infrastructure continues to grow across the region.PCL’s scope of work includes the phased construction of five steel-frame colocation data halls totaling approximately 277,000 square feet, allowing for efficient onboarding of new tenants while maintaining flexibility for future expansion. The project also features a dedicated administration building with office space for personnel and on-site operations teams. This design aims to accommodate high-density deployments, aligning performance needs with modern sustainability and energy-efficiency requirements.The project includes comprehensive electrical infrastructure to support continuous, high-performance operations, from permanent and temporary power systems to sitewide distribution, security, and communications pathways. Key work includes installing and integrating major power and cooling equipment that enables reliable, high-density data center operations. PCL Industrial Services, Inc. is self-performing the core electrical work while coordinating specialized partners for testing and life-safety systems.PCL Construction’s extensive experience in delivering mission-critical and data center facilities across the United States is critical on complex, high-density projects like this, according to Ryan Schmidt, vice president and district manager of Denver Buildings. The project teams recently marked the topping-out ceremony, celebrating the dedication, craftsmanship, and collaboration that will transition the development into interior build-out and systems installation, with construction scheduled for completion in summer 2027.

JE Dunn Construction has launched its new off-site manufacturing initiative, Form Off-Site Solutions(TradeMark), designed to specialize in Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. + JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION CELEBRATES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE FOR WORKFORCE CENTER

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — Construction has reached a key milestone on the Northland Workforce Development Center, as JE Dunn Construction and project partners recently celebrated the topping out of the new facility. The event, held on 19th March 2026, marked the placement of the final structural steel beam for the project, signaling significant progress on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story building designed to expand access to career and technical education across the Northland region.Prior to the beam’s installation, attendees participated in a traditional beam-signing ceremony, adding their names and messages to a steel beam bearing project and partner logos. Once complete, the facility will expand the capacity of Northland Career Center and its partners, serving both high school students and adult learners seeking industry-aligned training and credentials. The Northland Workforce Development Center represents a regional investment in workforce readiness, aligning educational infrastructure with evolving industry needs and supporting long-term economic development in the Kansas City area.

Once completed, the 14-story hospital and five-story pavilion will house operating rooms, an imaging center, and updated facilities dedicated to pharmacy and burn care units. + AUDDIA AND THRAMANN MERGE TO ESTABLISH MCCARTHY FINNEY HOLDING

Auddia Inc. has announced a definitive merger agreement with Thramann Holdings to form a new Delaware holding company named McCarthy Finney, Inc. The announcement was made on 17th February 2026, and the details of the merger were disclosed on 18th February 2026. Under this agreement, both Auddia and Thramann will become wholly owned subsidiaries of McCarthy Finney, with Auddia shareholders receiving one common share of McCarthy Finney for each share they hold in Auddia, while Thramann owners will receive a combination of special preferred stock and $3.5 million in unsecured notes.Post-merger, Auddia shareholders are projected to own approximately 20% of McCarthy Finney, while former Thramann stakeholders are expected to hold around 80%, contingent on Auddia maintaining a minimum cash balance of $12 million. The special preferred stock issued to Thramann will carry significant governance rights, including a liquidation preference of at least $20.5 million, alongside flexible conversion and redemption features, giving Thramann substantial influence over the newly formed entity.Both Auddia and Thramann's boards have unanimously approved the merger, with Auddia's independent special committee affirming the deal's fairness, supported by a fairness opinion from Houlihan Capital. Governance continuity is assured, with Auddia's four directors set to join the McCarthy Finney board, while Jeff Thramann and John Mahoney will continue as CEO and CFO, respectively. Additionally, Auddia's executives and directors have committed to support the deal, and Thramann will adhere to a 180-day lock-up on his shares in McCarthy Finney.In terms of valuation, Auddia estimates a base-case worth of $250 million for McCarthy Finney, based on a management-prepared discounted cash flow analysis of the combined entity's pro forma over the next decade. The merger is anticipated to close in the second quarter of 2026, pending Auddia shareholder approval, SEC effectiveness of a Form S-4 filing, and meeting Nasdaq listing requirements. The formation of McCarthy Finney positions the company as a diversified holding entity focused on artificial intelligence applications across distributed AI data centers, healthtech value-based care, and AI-driven travel services.Auddia, based in Boulder, Colorado, is recognized for its innovative audio technology that leverages a proprietary AI platform to enhance consumer engagement with AM/FM radio, podcasts, and other audio content. Its flagship product, the 'faidr' superapp, aims to modernize music consumption and promotion by offering ad-free listening, content and ad-break skipping, and integrated artist discovery.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS ADVANCES SUSTAINABILITY WITH 2024 GHG EMISSIONS REPORT

McCarthy Holdings, one of the largest employee-owned construction firms in the U.S., has released its updated 2024 Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emissions Report. The report was published on 16th February 2026, and reflects McCarthy's commitment to strengthening its sustainability efforts across its operations and subsidiaries.Developed in partnership with Gravity, a leading enterprise carbon accounting and energy management platform, the report provides a comprehensive view of McCarthy's carbon footprint. It covers Scope 1, Scope 2, and key categories of Scope 3 emissions, with Scopes 1 and 2 having undergone independent auditing and awarded limited assurance. This commitment to transparency and accountability aligns with McCarthy's mission to be the best builder in America.Ian Pope, Senior Director of Sustainability at McCarthy Holdings, emphasized the company's dedication to community building. 'As an employee-owned company, this important effort is in line with our core values and provides our teams with the practical data necessary to meet regulatory requirements,' he stated. McCarthy's sustainability strategy is anchored in three primary priorities: understanding its environmental impact, acting responsibly for its people and communities, and efficiently managing energy consumption.In addition to emissions reporting, McCarthy Holdings integrates responsible practices into its projects. This includes biodiversity initiatives, responsible fuel management, and the use of digital tools and artificial intelligence for smarter project decisions. The company is also focusing on responsible end-of-life management for solar panels through its advanced PV recycling program, partnering with Solar Energy Industries Association (SEIA)-approved recyclers to divert waste from landfills and support a circular economy.By leveraging emissions data and operational insights, McCarthy Holdings continues to demonstrate that its role as a builder of communities extends to fostering a more sustainable future. With a commitment to environmental stewardship and community well-being, McCarthy is setting benchmarks in the construction industry for responsible practices.
