urn:contentItem:6JH0-VC63-RX36-R32T-00000-00IKEA GROUP - PEER NEWS AND THE RECENT UPDATES2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:39:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKIKEA GROUP - PEER NEWS AND THE RECENT UPDATES

DASHBOARD: IKEA GROUP

PermID5001428642Websitehttp://www.ikea.com/us/en/IndustryFurniture & Home Goods RetailAddressLEIDEN ZUID-HOLLAND Netherlands

ACTIVITIES:

IKEA Group, founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad, is a global home furnishings retailer headquartered in Delft, Netherlands. Employing over 225,000 people across 50+ countries, it operates 460+ stores, generating 45 billion annually. Known for affordable, flat-pack furniture and Scandinavian design, IKEA serves millions with products for home living. In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

+ IKEA GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ IKEA FINLAND LAUNCHES DISABILITY-FOCUSED PILOT AT VANTAA STORE

IKEA Group has launched an accessibility pilot at its Vantaa store in Finland, aiming to improve the shopping experience for customers with disabilities. The program began on 28th April 2026 and combines assisted-shopping services with app-based navigation to bridge digital planning and in-store purchases. Two core features are being tested: a pre-booked 90-minute assisted-shopping session with a trained colleague providing personalized support, and the NaviLens app, which lets customers scan store codes to receive audio guidance on product areas and departmental locations. Both options are free and let shoppers choose their level of help. "We want as many people as possible to experience shopping at IKEA as smoothly as possible, based on their own needs," says Catharina Van Den Houwe, Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Leader for IKEA Group in Finland. The company says the pilot is a learning platform to gather feedback and refine the services for possible rollout elsewhere, as part of an ongoing push to create inclusive journeys online and in-store. The effort is supported by IKEA's Digital IDEA Centre of Expertise, and the broader context includes the World Health Organization estimate that about 1.3 billion people globally live with a significant disability.

+ IKEA UNVEILS STARKVIND: A DUAL-PURPOSE AIR PURIFIER AND SIDE TABLE

IKEA has revealed a bold new product that could reshape how people view home appliances. The Starkvind is a smart air purifier that also serves as a side table, offering a stylish way to boost indoor air quality. The announcement comes as many households spend more time at home and growing concerns about air cleanliness persist in light of wildfires in California. The Starkvind launch is set for 13th October 2021.As part of the Home Smart family, the Starkvind is designed to blend with modern interiors. When connected to the Tradfri Gateway, it can be controlled with the IKEA app on a smartphone, providing convenience without compromising style.The unit features a three stage filtration system that captures dust, hair and finer pollutants such as pollen. With five adjustable fan speeds and an auto mode that adapts to real time air quality, it works to maintain a healthier indoor environment. A built in sensor sends continuous air quality updates to the companion app, helping households monitor indoor air health.

+ IKEA CANADA EXTENDS 18-YEAR RUN AS A GREEN LEADER

BURLINGTON, ON – IKEA Canada has been recognized as one of Canada’s Greenest Employers for the 18th consecutive year, reflecting its long-standing commitment to sustainability and its 7,000-plus co-workers. The award, presented by Mediacorp Canada Inc., honors organizations that foster environmental stewardship across operations—from retail and supply chain to offices and fulfillment networks.Peter Jones, Head of Sustainability, said that sustainability is integral to the business: "Sustainability isn't something we do on the side, it's integrated throughout the business." He noted that each unit is assigned specific targets and that measurement drives progress, adding, "When you measure, you learn. And when you learn, you improve." IKEA Canada's initiatives include energy-efficient building upgrades, waste-reduction programs, and circular services to extend the life of home furnishings, with every location developing its own action plan within the broader Sustainability strategy.The organization celebrates its continued recognition on Canada’s Greenest Employers list, a result, according to Jones, of dedicated co-workers. "This recognition belongs to our co-workers," he said, underscoring the daily efforts behind IKEA Canada's leadership in sustainability. The date of this announcement is 21st April 2026.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ THE HOME DEPOT [3 News That Matters]

+ HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) DEPLOYS AI VOICE AGENTS TO IMPROVE CUSTOMER PHONE SUPPORT

As of 28th April 2026, Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) is deploying AI-powered phone agents built on Google Cloud's Gemini Enterprise for Customer Experience platform to help customers reach a Home Depot store by phone in a more intuitive way, allowing them to describe their needs in their own wordsThe AI voice agents are designed to instantly comprehend inquiries while preserving an option for direct human interaction. Jordan Broggi, executive VP of customer experience and president of online at Home Depot, notes that high call volumes have diverted associates from assisting in-store shoppers, and the new technology aims to improve both customer and associate experiences by better handling phone inquiriesHome Depot selected Gemini for its advanced capabilities in understanding conversation and intent, with AI models that integrate with existing systems to deliver a cohesive customer experience. Customers benefit from faster resolutions, with the system understanding intent in ten seconds and often resolving requests in a single interaction. Pilot feedback indicates increased job satisfaction among associates, who can devote more time to in-store service, and the company plans to roll out the program to all U.S. stores by the end of 2026, guided by ongoing customer feedback

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE: HD) POSTS DEBUT IN SAN MARCOS WITH $13 MILLION STORE PROJECT

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE: HD) Posts Debut in San Marcos With a $13 Million Store Project, Confirmed on 28th April 2026 The project marks the retailer's first location in this rapidly growing college town, which has seen an influx of residents and retail opportunities in recent years. Construction plans have been filed with the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR), and the footprint totals 135,918 square feet including a 107,797-square-foot main store and a 28,120-square-foot garden center, with construction slated to begin in September 2026 and finish in August 2027. The site sits near a third H-E-B grocery store currently under construction and expected to open in October 2026. San Marcos lies on the I-35 corridor beside Texas State University, fueling growth in housing, retail and infrastructure. The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE: HD) also reported a 3.2% increase in fiscal year 2025 and operates approximately 2,359 locations across the United States, reflecting its ongoing expansion in active markets

+ AUTOZONE, INC. (NYSE:AZO) JOINS GOOGLE IN AGENTIC AI PUSH ACROSS RETAIL

During the Google Cloud Next 2026 conference in Las Vegas on 23rd April 2026, AutoZone, The Home Depot, Macy's and Ulta Beauty announced partnerships with Google to launch agentic artificial intelligence services across their digital and store ecosystems. The four retailers aim to harness Google AI to improve customer interactions and streamline operations.AutoZone, Inc. (NYSE:AZO) completed a full-stack migration to Google Cloud, planning to retire legacy data centers within three years. The move is designed to boost performance and flexibility, and AutoZone will use Google AI Cloud to improve system monitoring and automate high-volume tasks, raising productivity.The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) will roll out AI-powered phone agents built on Google Cloud Gemini Enterprise for Customer Experience, enabling natural language conversations that cut down on menu navigation. Early pilots show inquiries resolved in under ten seconds, with a full rollout across U.S. stores planned in the coming year. Macy's, Inc. (NYSE:M) introduced Ask Macy's, a Google Gemini-based shopping assistant on the site and app, offering image-driven virtual try-ons and personalized size recommendations. Ulta Beauty, Inc. (NASDAQ:ULTA) is launching Ulta AI and collaborating on the Universal Commerce Protocol for agentic AI-based commerce to make beauty discovery more seamless across digital channels

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA [3 News That Matters]

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE:WSM) PROMOTES AUJSHA TAYLOR TO LEAD REJUVENATION

On Apr. 14, 2026, Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE:WSM) promotes Aujsha Taylor to president of Rejuvenation. Taylor joined Williams-Sonoma in 2003 as a district manager for Pottery Barn and has since risen through the Rejuvenation banner, most recently serving as senior vice president since 2020.In her new role, Taylor will oversee merchandising strategies, product innovation, and growth initiatives designed to position Rejuvenation as a stronger pillar within the Williams-Sonoma portfolio. CEO Laura Alber said Taylor’s leadership has helped pave the way for Rejuvenation to become a billion-dollar brand, noting her track record, operational excellence, and passion for design.Rejuvenation has expanded its retail footprint with new stores in Cary, North Carolina, and Salt Lake City, Utah, and has pursued collaborations such as a lighting collection with Roll & Hill. Williams-Sonoma has also named Vicki McWilliams as president of stores and customer care and Abby Teisch as chief marketing officer. The company continues to expect 2026 net revenue growth of 2.7% to 6.7%.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE: WSM) REAFFIRMS HOLD RATING AS EVERCORE RAISES TARGET TO $200

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE: WSM) Reaffirms Hold Rating as Evercore Raises Target to $200 from $195 in a 14th April 2026 update. Evercore maintains its Hold rating on Williams-Sonoma, Inc. while lifting the price target to $200, reflecting a reassessment of growth potential in a competitive home-goods market. Analyst Oliver Wintermantel, who covers Williams-Sonoma at Evercore, has a 66.7% success rate and a 23.9% average return over the past year, according to TipRanks. The hold rating suggests a cautious stance as seasonal trends, inventory management and supply-chain efficiency will influence whether Williams-Sonoma can meet the $200 target in the coming quarters. Evercore's raised target provides investors a reference point in a competitive market.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE: WSM) RECEIVES BUY RATING FROM GOLDMAN SACHS

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE: WSM) marks an upgrade on 13th April 2026 as Goldman Sachs moves its rating from Neutral to Buy, citing a positive outlook for the home goods retailer. Goldman Sachs sets a one-year price target for Williams-Sonoma at $208.74 per share, implying a potential upside of 7.91% from the latest close of $193.43. The firm lists price forecasts ranging from a low of $139.60 to a high of $241.50, illustrating a broad spectrum of investor expectations.Based on the projections, Williams-Sonoma is expected to generate about $8.29 billion in annual revenue, up 6.20% versus prior estimates. However, non-GAAP earnings per share are forecast to be $13.00, down 33.82% from earlier projections.This upgrade aligns with a broader reassessment of the home goods sector as consumers adjust spending patterns. Williams-Sonoma, a leading player in home furnishings, could benefit from ongoing home-improvement and decor investments as the new financial year approaches.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ AMAZON EXPANDS NORTHWEST FOOTPRINT WITH $200 MILLION SPANAWAY DISTRIBUTION COMPLEX ACQUISITION

Amazon has acquired Ashley Furniture Industries' Spanaway distribution complex—a 1.1 million square-foot facility and surrounding parcels—for more than $200 million, a deal reported on the 27th of April 2026.The transaction fits Amazon's ongoing effort to streamline its supply chain and bolster warehousing capacity, a priority as e-commerce grows and customers demand faster deliveries in the Pacific Northwest.Ashley Furniture Industries, a major player in home furnishings, sold the Spanaway property as part of a strategic realignment. The sale may indicate a shift in the company's distribution focus, with implications for its future logistics strategy.

+ FRONTLINEIQ UNVEILS ASHLEY AI ADVANTAGE AT HIGH POINT MARKET, INTRODUCING REAL-TIME SALES COACHING

HIGH POINT—FrontlineIQ(TradeMark), the world’s leading AI sales coaching platform purpose-built for in-person sales teams, today announced the Ashley AI Advantage—developed in partnership with Ashley Furniture Industries. The launch occurred at the High Point Spring Market on the 27th of April, 2026, signaling a new era for the home furnishings sector.The Ashley AI Advantage introduces interactive AI role-play and real-time coaching designed for frontline associates, enabling practice with real-world customer interactions and instant feedback based on proven sales methodologies. The program is offered free of charge to Ashley Wholesale customers and Ashley licensees, with options for fully customized deployments for retailers who want to align the solution with their own training playbooks.This rollout positions Ashley as a scalable entry point to FrontlineIQ's platform, pre-configured for the home furnishings industry and available at no cost to Ashley partners. Ben Rodier, CEO of FrontlineIQ, said, "This is bigger than coaching—it’s about unlocking the full potential of every frontline sales associate. Partnering with Ashley to bring this to market is a defining moment—not just for our companies, but for retail as a whole."

+ ORNATE HOME EXPANDS COLLABORATION WITH ASHLEY FURNITURE FOLLOWING NC FACILITY TOUR

SANTA ANA, CA – Ornate Home's founder, Mehmet Uncuoglu, recently completed a tour of Ashley Furniture Industries’ manufacturing and distribution facility in Advance, North Carolina, during the 21st of April, 2026 visit, a step in strengthening the relationship between the growing furniture and mattress retailer and one of the world's largest manufacturers. The trip provided him with firsthand insights into large-scale production and distribution that will support service delivery at Ornate Home's Santa Ana and Charlotte showrooms. The tour encompassed production floors, logistics operations, quality control processes, and supply chain coordination at the Advance hub, one of Ashley's major U.S. centers. Ashley Furniture Industries, which employs more than 35,000 team members worldwide and operates over 30 million square feet of manufacturing and distribution space globally, arranged the visit to emphasize observational learning and relationship building between the two companies. During the tour, Uncuoglu examined processes from raw material handling through final product distribution, observing an integrated approach that blends traditional craftsmanship with modern manufacturing technology and logistics systems serving retailers across North America. 'The Ashley team’s hospitality and transparency during our visit was remarkable,' said Mehmet Uncuoglu. 'Seeing their commitment to quality control and efficiency reinforces why Ashley furniture and mattress products remain cornerstone offerings in our showrooms.' The visit aligns with Ornate Home's expansion plans, particularly as the Charlotte location benefits from a regional link to Ashley's North Carolina operations, enabling faster delivery times. Ornate Home operates as an Ashley furniture store serving Santa Ana and Charlotte customers. Founded in 2021, Ornate Home provides same-day white-glove delivery throughout Orange County and ships nationwide from company-owned warehouses.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES [3 News That Matters]

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) POSTS FIVE NEW STORE OPENINGS LED BY PORT ST. LUCIE

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Posts Five New Store Openings Led by Port St. LucieLowe's will open the first of five stores planned for 2026, starting with a Port St. Lucie, Florida location on April 24, 2026. The Port St. Lucie store will offer approximately 94,000 square feet of retail space, a 30,000-square-foot garden centre, and will employ more than 100 associates. It will be Lowe's second location in Port St. Lucie and will feature redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms designed to blend style, affordability and innovation. New Lowe's showrooms are standard for all new stores and will be introduced to a limited number of existing Lowe’s stores in 2026. New Lowe's stores will also include self-service paint kiosks for streamlined paint ordering and buy online, pick up in-store zones.Beyond Port St. Lucie, Lowe's has four other openings planned in Kaufman, W. Katy, Willis, Texas, and Walton, Kentucky later this year. This expansion supports Lowe's broader initiative to meet the rising demand for home improvement products as housing markets rebound. Redman noted that Port St. Lucie is growing rapidly and the new store is built to deliver value, convenience and the service the community expects from Lowe's. Investors will watch how these locations contribute to overall sales growth and customer engagement in a competitive retail landscape.

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) EXPANDS U.S. FOOTPRINT WITH NEW STORE OPENINGS

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) expands its U.S. footprint with new store openings, including a Florida location slated for early June 2026 and four additional sites in Texas and Kentucky expected to begin operations later this year. The Port St. Lucie store will span about 94,000 square feet and feature a 30,000-square-foot garden center, employing more than 100 staff members, and will incorporate redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms, self-service kiosks for paint orders, and dedicated areas for online purchases with in-store pickup.Lowe's currently operates over 1,700 home improvement stores nationwide, along with 530 branches and 130 distribution centers, handling roughly 16 million transactions each week. In fiscal 2024, sales surpassed $83 billion. The retailer also emphasizes its MyLowe's Rewards program, which offers points on eligible purchases and tiered memberships based on spending, supporting loyalty and repeat business, while continuing community initiatives aimed at safe, affordable housing and workforce development

+ LOWE’S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) EXPANDS FOOTPRINT WITH PORT ST. LUCIE STORE AND TEXAS OPENINGS

Apr. 21, 2026 Lowe’s Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) is expanding its footprint with a new Port St. Lucie, Florida store, the 133rd location in the state. The 94,000-square-foot facility includes a 30,000-square-foot garden center and schedules an early June grand opening as Lowe’s launches five new stores this year.The Port St. Lucie location will create over 100 jobs and signals Lowe’s commitment to growing markets. In addition to Port St. Lucie, Lowe’s has slated openings in Kaufman, W. Katy, Willis, Texas, and Walton, Kentucky, as part of a broader expansion to serve a diverse customer base. The retailer will redesign kitchen and appliance showrooms to blend style with affordability across new and select existing locations in 2026. Christian Redman, Lowe’s district manager, stated, "Port St. Lucie is growing rapidly, and we’re excited to support our neighbors and local businesses with everything they need for projects big and small." Lowe’s operates more than 1,700 stores across North America

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TARGET [3 News That Matters]

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) EXPANDS NEXT-DAY DELIVERY TO 60% OF U.S. POPULATION, REACHES 50 MAJOR METRO AREAS

As Of The 28th Of April 2026, Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) Expands Next-Day Delivery To 60% Of The U.S. Population, Reaching More Than 50 Major Metropolitan Areas, With Many Deliveries Offered FreeThe Rollout Increases The Eligible Item Pool To 85% Of All Products Sold In Stores, And Allows Customers To Place Orders As Late As 6 P.M. Local Time For Next-Day Arrival, While Standard Delivery Is $5.99 And Free Delivery Applies To Orders Over $35 In Eligible MarketsThis Initiative Is Part Of The New Chapter $5 Billion Strategy Led By Chief Executive Officer Michael Fiddelke, Aimed At Refreshing Stores, Launching New Collaborations, And Strengthening Local Communities Following A 1.7% Net Sales Decline In 2025

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) E-COMMERCE SHARE REACHES 20.6% OF MERCHANDISE SALES

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) posts fiscal 2025 results for the period ending 31st January 2026, with the stock up 29% since the start of 2026.\n\nE-commerce sales reach 20.6% of total merchandise sales, a record share, up from nearly 18% during the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020. In the fourth quarter, e-commerce represented 23.7% of merchandise sales.\n\nNon-merchandise sales rose 25% in the same quarter, driven by membership revenue which more than doubled year over year. Target operates as a general merchandise retailer with physical stores and digital platforms, blending everyday essentials with discounted differentiated merchandise to attract customers. Analysts note that opportunities in AI and other sectors may offer upside

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) EXPANDS BULLSEYE BUILDS WITH COMMUNITY PROGRAM ACROSS 13 CITIES BACKED BY $1 MILLION INVESTMENT

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) leads the Bullseye Builds with Community Program, a design-driven effort to transform community spaces across 13 U.S. cities. Backed by a $1 million investment, the rollout will officially kick off on 21st May 2026 in Denver and Las VegasThe program, launched in 2024, has completed 25 projects to date and has positively impacted over 250,000 community members with the help of Target team volunteers. In Denver, Target will partner with Trevista at Horace Mann Elementary School to upgrade the student wellness room and create outdoor learning spaces, featuring murals and new furniture. In Las Vegas, Target will team with The Just One Project to address food insecurity and enhance shopping areas and pantry facilities. Each project will involve more than 100 volunteers from TargetAdditional cities slated for this year’s Bullseye Builds include Austin, Birmingham, Columbus, Jersey City, Logan Township, Orlando, Washington, D.C.; Minneapolis-St. Paul; Phoenix; St. Louis; and Seattle. Kiera Fernandez, Executive Vice President and Chief Community and Stakeholder Engagement Officer, emphasized the importance of local insights in shaping these projects: "At Target, we believe investing in the places we call home builds strong communities and strong partnerships." The company also noted that team members contributed 1 million volunteer hours in the U.S. in 2025

urn:contentItem:6JGY-HMB3-SD84-W3J7-00000-00MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - INDUSTRY UPDATES FROM PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:39:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKMCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - INDUSTRY UPDATES FROM PEERS

DASHBOARD: MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC.

PermID4296686586Websitehttp://www.mccarthy.com/IndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress1604 Eastport Plaza Drive, Suite 106 (Map it) Collinsville, Illinois 62234

ACTIVITIES:

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc., founded in 1864, is an employee-owned construction firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 7,000 employees, it provides preconstruction and construction services for healthcare, commercial, and renewable energy projects, generating $5.5 billion in 2023 revenue. Notable projects include the Mayo Clinic expansion. McCarthys focus on safety, quality, and technology, including BIM, ensures efficient project delivery. Serving clients like Kaiser Permanente, it competes with Skanska while fostering a collaborative culture and sustainability initiatives, maintaining a strong presence in the U.S. construction market.

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. [4 Press Releases]

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES TOPS OUT GILEAD SCIENCES' NEW TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT CENTER IN FOSTER CITY

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. (McCarthy), recently celebrated the topping out of the New Technical Development Center (NTDC) at Gilead Sciences' headquarters in Foster City, marking a major construction milestone with the completion of the building's structural frame. The work is in collaboration with design partners DGA Architects, Salas O'Brien Structural Engineers and civil designer BKF Engineer.

Project partners, field teams and leaders gathered on site to celebrate the center's topping out, marking placement of the final beam and the structure reaching full height. The centuries-old tradition honors the collective effort behind the milestone and signals that structural work is nearing completion as the project moves into its next phase.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: EXPANDING HOSPITAL PARKING STRUCTURES FOR BETTER CARE

Driving your child to the hospital is already a harrowing experience. Finding parking once you arrive should not be your biggest concern. When parking structures prioritize flexibility, enhance connectivity, and respond to evolving patient and staff needs, they make hospital visits a little less daunting.

In early 2025, as McCarthy Building Companies completed the Southwest Tower at Children's Hospital of Orange County (CHOC), in Orange, California, the construction firm also expanded the facility's parking structure. This project involved expanding the structure from 522,000 to 912,000 square feet by adding three vertical levels as well as another horizontal bay to connect the new outpatient tower and the existing main campus. However, the expansion was about more than connecting buildings. It increased the efficiency and sustainability of the overall structure by accommodating a growing number of patients and staff within this care-centric environment.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: BRIGHT FUTURE: A'JEYA ANTHONY'S PATH FROM NSBE TO MCCARTHY

Our goal to be the best builder in America requires an inclusive talent acquisition strategy that enables us to find and recruit the best talent. By expanding our reach through organizations like the National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE), we can connect with emerging leaders and create more pathways into our industry.

A'Jeya Anthony is a three-time project intern at McCarthy Building Companies. Her journey is just one example of how meeting top candidates where they are help us to connect with exceptional people who strengthen our workforce and business.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. RYAN HOUSE DEBUTS TEEN SUITES FOR KIDS LIVING LONGER WITH LIFE-LIMITING CONDITIONS

Ryan House, a nonprofit that provides respite and end-of-life care to children with life-limiting conditions, recently capped off its 15th anniversary year by unveiling two newly renovated teen suites. The renovations allow Ryan House to better serve children who are now surviving well into adolescence and young adulthood and were made possible through generous support from McCarthy Building Companies, which selected Ryan House as a recipient of the company's Heart Hats(TradeMark) grants, and ARC Document Solutions.

Thanks to advances in diagnostics, disease management, and comprehensive care, many children with life-limiting conditions are now living longer than once expected. Ryan House is evolving alongside the children and families it serves, making meaningful changes to better support teens through environments designed specifically for this stage of life.

The renovation was spearheaded by McCarthy's Specialized Solutions Group, which focuses on complex renovations and technical builds within healthcare and other specialized environments. The donation involved the renovation of two private suites specifically designed for teenagers and features superhero-themed decor provided by ARC that fully immerses them in a comic book-inspired atmosphere. The rooms also now include fully adjustable desks to accommodate different mobility needs, allowing teens to comfortably do schoolwork or creative activities. Each suite is equipped with the latest state-of-the-art gaming system and adaptive controllers, plus customizable LED lighting that can change colors based on each teen's preferences. Additionally, bathroom updates were made to allow for enhanced comfort and accessibility.

"This renovation speaks to how pediatric care has changed over the past decade and a half," said Dr. Wendy Bernatavicius, medical director at Ryan House and division chief of primary, complex care and adolescent medicine at Phoenix Children's Hospital. "We're seeing more adolescents and young adults with complex needs living vibrant lives. Improved medical care, early intervention and whole-family support have extended the lifespan of many of these teens. That means rethinking the environments where they're cared for. These teen suites are not just rooms. They're an affirmation of identity, belonging, and respect for each young person who calls Ryan House their home away from home."

During their recent holiday break, Ryan House welcomed two familiar guests, brothers Mason and Jaxton, who are living with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, as the first teens to stay in the newly renovated suites. While they've stayed at Ryan House before, this visit marked the first time they experienced the upgraded rooms designed specifically with teens, like them, in mind.

"The gift of these teen suites is the culmination of our vision for this next chapter," said Tracy Leonard Warner, executive director of Ryan House. "This home has been serving families

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION HASLAM SPORTS GROUP, AECOM HUNT AND TURNER JV BREAK GROUND ON HUNTINGTON BANK FIELD

Construction of New State-of-the-Art Facility to Serve as Ohio's First Enclosed Stadium Revolutionary Design by HKS to Bring Football Fans Closer to the Field Than EverThe Haslam Sports Group (HSG) alongside a joint venture of AECOM Hunt and Turner Construction Company and HKS today announced the groundbreaking of the new Huntington Bank Field, the future home of the Cleveland Browns starting in the 2029 season. The groundbreaking ceremony held at the new site in Brook Park today marks the official start of construction on Northeast Ohio's largest economic development project to date, the new Huntington Bank Field enclosed stadium and surrounding district.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION WHERE SMALL CHANGES CREATE REAL IMPACT

Turner's support to the Paraplegic Foundation reflects a focus on improving everyday spaces at Sanjeevandeep Special School. The initiative has contributed to creating a more accessible, comfortable environment for children and caregivers.Our recent association with the Paraplegic Foundation came from a shared belief that everyone deserves the opportunity to live with dignity, comfort, and as much independence as possible. Through their work, the Foundation supports initiatives like Sanjeevandeep Special School, a space dedicated to children with special needs, where care, learning, and rehabilitation come together in a very real, hands-on way.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: CONNECTING YOUNG TALENT WITH INDUSTRY LEADERS

As part of our Young Professionals Employee Resource Group initiatives, we hosted the first session of our Coffee Talks with Industry Leaders series, with the participation of Arup Turkiye Country Manager Tulay Hatirnaz, Associate Director Duygu Tokalakoglu, and Associate Aydan Basyazicioglu. Held on April 10 at the Arup Istanbul office, the session brought together our young professionals from across Turkiye. Our esteemed speakers shared their industry experience, career journeys, and leadership perspectives, while also engaging with participants through an interactive Q&A.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION BREAKS GROUND ON $190 MILLION CONTRACT FOR STATE-OF-THE-ART DRINKING WATER FACILITY

DENVER, Colo. (Sept. 25, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. celebrated the groundbreaking of a $190 million contract to build the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility - an innovative project that employs advanced treatment technology to ensure clean water to residents.

The City of Westminster entrusted the PCL team to build the project's most critical components. As the lead contractor, PCL will manage the construction of cutting-edge filtration and treatment technologies. The facility will feature advanced oxidation and filtration systems designed to improve taste and odor, while enhancing resiliency against fluctuations in incoming water quality.

Designed with long-term sustainability in mind, the project replaces aging infrastructure and will provide reliable drinking water to nearly 33,000 homes and businesses. Once operational the facility will have the capacity to treat 14.7 million gallons of water per day (MGD).

"PCL is proud to celebrate the opening of the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility with the City of Westminster. This is an essential project that secures safe, reliable drinking water for the community," said Ankur Talwar, PCL's West region district manager.

The groundbreaking ceremony brought together Westminster City Council members, build partners, local leaders, and community members to celebrate the future site.

The facility is slated to be finished in 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION AND PHOENIX CHILDREN'S: BUILDING A STRONGER FUTURE FOR PEDIATRIC CARE

PHOENIX, Ariz. (November 10, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. and Phoenix Children's are proud to celebrate a partnership dedicated to advancing pediatric health and supporting children facing critical medical challenges in Arizona.

For eight consecutive years, PCL Construction in Arizona has deepened its commitment to Phoenix Children's, increasing its annual donation to $50,000 in support of pediatric cancer care. This ongoing collaboration directly benefits children by funding medical specialists, cutting-edge technology, and family-centered care.

"Our partnership with Phoenix Children's is about more than sponsorship-it's about building hope and supporting families during their most difficult times," said Josh Yates, construction risk manager at PCL Construction. "We are honored to contribute to the hospital's mission and to help create brighter futures for children in our community."

As part of this partnership, PCL proudly sponsors Phoenix Children's annual Big Dig for Kids event. This unique experience gives pediatric patients a chance to explore construction equipment, participate in hands-on activities, and enjoy a day filled with fun and inspiration. The event not only raises vital funds for pediatric cancer care but also brings moments of joy to children and families, helping them envision a future beyond illness.

Source: Company Website

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. DAVID CAMPBELL NAMED TO AZ BIG MEDIA'S 2026 "PEOPLE TO KNOW IN COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE" LIST

PHOENIX, Arizona (November 6, 2025) - PCL Buildings Inc. is pleased to announce that David Campbell, district manager for PCL Construction's Phoenix buildings office, has been named to AZ Big Media's "2026 People and Projects to Know in Commercial Real Estate" list. This annual recognition celebrates influential leaders who are shaping the future of Arizona's commercial real estate industry.

"This recognition highlights the momentum we're building in Arizona's construction market and our commitment to contributing positively to the region's future," says Campbell. "I'm proud to represent PCL and support the growth and innovation happening across Arizona."

Campbell was named as the leader of PCL's newest commercial buildings office in Phoenix this past spring, building upon a nearly 40-year legacy of excellence in water and civil construction across the Southwest. The Phoenix Buildings office marks a strategic milestone in PCL's commitment to the region, offering construction expertise across sectors including hospitality, manufacturing, aviation, and commercial and retail development.

His appearance on AZ Big Media's list reflects Campbell's contributions to the industry over the course of his nearly 20-year construction career, having led a wide range of high-profile projects across the U.S. Campbell joined PCL in 2005, with early contributions to entertainment and hospitality developments in Orlando. He later served as senior project manager on the American Dream megamall in New Jersey and held several leadership roles at PCL's U.S. head office in Denver before his current role in Phoenix.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE, LLC [2 Press Releases]

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE, LLC MOUNT SINAI MEDICAL CENTER REACHES TOPPING OUT MILESTONE FOR NEW WESTCHESTER EMERGENCY AND SPECIALTY CARE FACILITY

Construction crews have placed the final beam atop Mount Sinai Medical Center's new Westchester Freestanding Emergency Department, marking a major milestone in expanding access to high-quality, around-the-clock care for residents in western Miami-Dade County. The new facility will bring Mount Sinai's trusted emergency and specialty care closer to home for thousands of area families, helping reduce travel times and meet growing healthcare needs.

Located in Westchester, the four-story, 112,000 square-foot facility-designed by HuntonBrady Architects-will feature 26 exam bays, a Baker Act receiving unit, and advanced imaging services such as MRI, CT, and ultrasound. The project also features a detached two-story central energy plant and medical gas building, demolition of two existing structures and six acres of site improvements. Construction is expected to be complete late 2026.

"This milestone is a tribute to the skill and dedication of every crew member who's helped bring this facility to life," said Mike Peters, vice president and division manager at Brasfield & Gorrie. "Partnering with Mount Sinai Medical Center and HuntonBrady Architects on this important project highlights what can be achieved through teamwork and craftsmanship. Our crews take great pride in knowing their work will help make emergency and specialty care more accessible for families across Miami-Dade County."

Source: Company Website

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE TOPS OUT TRUELINE ENTERTAINMENT VENUE IN GREENVILLE

The Trueline Greenville project in South Carolina has reached its full height with over 270 tons of steel, marking a major construction milestone for the dynamic live entertainment and events venue opening in 2026. Trueline and Brasfield & Gorrie, the project's general contractor, celebrated the occasion with a topping out ceremony recognizing the craft workers, community partners, visionaries, and investors helping bring the project to life.

"Topping out marks an important milestone in any project, and this one stands out because of what it represents for Greenville," said Brasfield & Gorrie Vice President and Division Manager Ben Barfield. "Trueline will add a dynamic new chapter to Greenville's arts and entertainment scene, a place where people can connect through music, creativity, and community. We're proud to help bring this vision to life alongside our partners and talented craft professionals."

About Trueline Opening in 2026, Trueline Greenville is a dynamic live entertainment venue located in the city's vibrant West End district. Featuring a 1,700-capacity main hall, a 350-capacity room for emerging artists, a private members' club with a rooftop terrace, and a clubhouse, and courtyard. Trueline blends high-level hospitality with world-class production. Built in partnership with Brasfield & Gorrie, Trueline is designed to inspire connection through music, art, and inspiring performances, creating a must-play venue in a must-see city.

Source: Company Website

DASHBOARD: MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC.

PermID4296686586Websitehttp://www.mccarthy.com/IndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress1604 Eastport Plaza Drive, Suite 106 (Map it) Collinsville, Illinois 62234

ACTIVITIES:

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc., founded in 1864, is an employee-owned construction firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 7,000 employees, it provides preconstruction and construction services for healthcare, commercial, and renewable energy projects, generating $5.5 billion in 2023 revenue. Notable projects include the Mayo Clinic expansion. McCarthys focus on safety, quality, and technology, including BIM, ensures efficient project delivery. Serving clients like Kaiser Permanente, it competes with Skanska while fostering a collaborative culture and sustainability initiatives, maintaining a strong presence in the U.S. construction market.

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. [4 Press Releases]

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES TOPS OUT GILEAD SCIENCES' NEW TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT CENTER IN FOSTER CITY

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. (McCarthy), recently celebrated the topping out of the New Technical Development Center (NTDC) at Gilead Sciences' headquarters in Foster City, marking a major construction milestone with the completion of the building's structural frame. The work is in collaboration with design partners DGA Architects, Salas O'Brien Structural Engineers and civil designer BKF Engineer.

Project partners, field teams and leaders gathered on site to celebrate the center's topping out, marking placement of the final beam and the structure reaching full height. The centuries-old tradition honors the collective effort behind the milestone and signals that structural work is nearing completion as the project moves into its next phase.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: EXPANDING HOSPITAL PARKING STRUCTURES FOR BETTER CARE

Driving your child to the hospital is already a harrowing experience. Finding parking once you arrive should not be your biggest concern. When parking structures prioritize flexibility, enhance connectivity, and respond to evolving patient and staff needs, they make hospital visits a little less daunting.

In early 2025, as McCarthy Building Companies completed the Southwest Tower at Children's Hospital of Orange County (CHOC), in Orange, California, the construction firm also expanded the facility's parking structure. This project involved expanding the structure from 522,000 to 912,000 square feet by adding three vertical levels as well as another horizontal bay to connect the new outpatient tower and the existing main campus. However, the expansion was about more than connecting buildings. It increased the efficiency and sustainability of the overall structure by accommodating a growing number of patients and staff within this care-centric environment.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: BRIGHT FUTURE: A'JEYA ANTHONY'S PATH FROM NSBE TO MCCARTHY

Our goal to be the best builder in America requires an inclusive talent acquisition strategy that enables us to find and recruit the best talent. By expanding our reach through organizations like the National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE), we can connect with emerging leaders and create more pathways into our industry.

A'Jeya Anthony is a three-time project intern at McCarthy Building Companies. Her journey is just one example of how meeting top candidates where they are help us to connect with exceptional people who strengthen our workforce and business.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. RYAN HOUSE DEBUTS TEEN SUITES FOR KIDS LIVING LONGER WITH LIFE-LIMITING CONDITIONS

Ryan House, a nonprofit that provides respite and end-of-life care to children with life-limiting conditions, recently capped off its 15th anniversary year by unveiling two newly renovated teen suites. The renovations allow Ryan House to better serve children who are now surviving well into adolescence and young adulthood and were made possible through generous support from McCarthy Building Companies, which selected Ryan House as a recipient of the company's Heart Hats(TradeMark) grants, and ARC Document Solutions.

Thanks to advances in diagnostics, disease management, and comprehensive care, many children with life-limiting conditions are now living longer than once expected. Ryan House is evolving alongside the children and families it serves, making meaningful changes to better support teens through environments designed specifically for this stage of life.

The renovation was spearheaded by McCarthy's Specialized Solutions Group, which focuses on complex renovations and technical builds within healthcare and other specialized environments. The donation involved the renovation of two private suites specifically designed for teenagers and features superhero-themed decor provided by ARC that fully immerses them in a comic book-inspired atmosphere. The rooms also now include fully adjustable desks to accommodate different mobility needs, allowing teens to comfortably do schoolwork or creative activities. Each suite is equipped with the latest state-of-the-art gaming system and adaptive controllers, plus customizable LED lighting that can change colors based on each teen's preferences. Additionally, bathroom updates were made to allow for enhanced comfort and accessibility.

"This renovation speaks to how pediatric care has changed over the past decade and a half," said Dr. Wendy Bernatavicius, medical director at Ryan House and division chief of primary, complex care and adolescent medicine at Phoenix Children's Hospital. "We're seeing more adolescents and young adults with complex needs living vibrant lives. Improved medical care, early intervention and whole-family support have extended the lifespan of many of these teens. That means rethinking the environments where they're cared for. These teen suites are not just rooms. They're an affirmation of identity, belonging, and respect for each young person who calls Ryan House their home away from home."

During their recent holiday break, Ryan House welcomed two familiar guests, brothers Mason and Jaxton, who are living with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, as the first teens to stay in the newly renovated suites. While they've stayed at Ryan House before, this visit marked the first time they experienced the upgraded rooms designed specifically with teens, like them, in mind.

"The gift of these teen suites is the culmination of our vision for this next chapter," said Tracy Leonard Warner, executive director of Ryan House. "This home has been serving families

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION HASLAM SPORTS GROUP, AECOM HUNT AND TURNER JV BREAK GROUND ON HUNTINGTON BANK FIELD

Construction of New State-of-the-Art Facility to Serve as Ohio's First Enclosed Stadium Revolutionary Design by HKS to Bring Football Fans Closer to the Field Than EverThe Haslam Sports Group (HSG) alongside a joint venture of AECOM Hunt and Turner Construction Company and HKS today announced the groundbreaking of the new Huntington Bank Field, the future home of the Cleveland Browns starting in the 2029 season. The groundbreaking ceremony held at the new site in Brook Park today marks the official start of construction on Northeast Ohio's largest economic development project to date, the new Huntington Bank Field enclosed stadium and surrounding district.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION WHERE SMALL CHANGES CREATE REAL IMPACT

Turner's support to the Paraplegic Foundation reflects a focus on improving everyday spaces at Sanjeevandeep Special School. The initiative has contributed to creating a more accessible, comfortable environment for children and caregivers.Our recent association with the Paraplegic Foundation came from a shared belief that everyone deserves the opportunity to live with dignity, comfort, and as much independence as possible. Through their work, the Foundation supports initiatives like Sanjeevandeep Special School, a space dedicated to children with special needs, where care, learning, and rehabilitation come together in a very real, hands-on way.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: CONNECTING YOUNG TALENT WITH INDUSTRY LEADERS

As part of our Young Professionals Employee Resource Group initiatives, we hosted the first session of our Coffee Talks with Industry Leaders series, with the participation of Arup Turkiye Country Manager Tulay Hatirnaz, Associate Director Duygu Tokalakoglu, and Associate Aydan Basyazicioglu. Held on April 10 at the Arup Istanbul office, the session brought together our young professionals from across Turkiye. Our esteemed speakers shared their industry experience, career journeys, and leadership perspectives, while also engaging with participants through an interactive Q&A.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION BREAKS GROUND ON $190 MILLION CONTRACT FOR STATE-OF-THE-ART DRINKING WATER FACILITY

DENVER, Colo. (Sept. 25, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. celebrated the groundbreaking of a $190 million contract to build the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility - an innovative project that employs advanced treatment technology to ensure clean water to residents.

The City of Westminster entrusted the PCL team to build the project's most critical components. As the lead contractor, PCL will manage the construction of cutting-edge filtration and treatment technologies. The facility will feature advanced oxidation and filtration systems designed to improve taste and odor, while enhancing resiliency against fluctuations in incoming water quality.

Designed with long-term sustainability in mind, the project replaces aging infrastructure and will provide reliable drinking water to nearly 33,000 homes and businesses. Once operational the facility will have the capacity to treat 14.7 million gallons of water per day (MGD).

"PCL is proud to celebrate the opening of the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility with the City of Westminster. This is an essential project that secures safe, reliable drinking water for the community," said Ankur Talwar, PCL's West region district manager.

The groundbreaking ceremony brought together Westminster City Council members, build partners, local leaders, and community members to celebrate the future site.

The facility is slated to be finished in 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION AND PHOENIX CHILDREN'S: BUILDING A STRONGER FUTURE FOR PEDIATRIC CARE

PHOENIX, Ariz. (November 10, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. and Phoenix Children's are proud to celebrate a partnership dedicated to advancing pediatric health and supporting children facing critical medical challenges in Arizona.

For eight consecutive years, PCL Construction in Arizona has deepened its commitment to Phoenix Children's, increasing its annual donation to $50,000 in support of pediatric cancer care. This ongoing collaboration directly benefits children by funding medical specialists, cutting-edge technology, and family-centered care.

"Our partnership with Phoenix Children's is about more than sponsorship-it's about building hope and supporting families during their most difficult times," said Josh Yates, construction risk manager at PCL Construction. "We are honored to contribute to the hospital's mission and to help create brighter futures for children in our community."

As part of this partnership, PCL proudly sponsors Phoenix Children's annual Big Dig for Kids event. This unique experience gives pediatric patients a chance to explore construction equipment, participate in hands-on activities, and enjoy a day filled with fun and inspiration. The event not only raises vital funds for pediatric cancer care but also brings moments of joy to children and families, helping them envision a future beyond illness.

Source: Company Website

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. DAVID CAMPBELL NAMED TO AZ BIG MEDIA'S 2026 "PEOPLE TO KNOW IN COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE" LIST

PHOENIX, Arizona (November 6, 2025) - PCL Buildings Inc. is pleased to announce that David Campbell, district manager for PCL Construction's Phoenix buildings office, has been named to AZ Big Media's "2026 People and Projects to Know in Commercial Real Estate" list. This annual recognition celebrates influential leaders who are shaping the future of Arizona's commercial real estate industry.

"This recognition highlights the momentum we're building in Arizona's construction market and our commitment to contributing positively to the region's future," says Campbell. "I'm proud to represent PCL and support the growth and innovation happening across Arizona."

Campbell was named as the leader of PCL's newest commercial buildings office in Phoenix this past spring, building upon a nearly 40-year legacy of excellence in water and civil construction across the Southwest. The Phoenix Buildings office marks a strategic milestone in PCL's commitment to the region, offering construction expertise across sectors including hospitality, manufacturing, aviation, and commercial and retail development.

His appearance on AZ Big Media's list reflects Campbell's contributions to the industry over the course of his nearly 20-year construction career, having led a wide range of high-profile projects across the U.S. Campbell joined PCL in 2005, with early contributions to entertainment and hospitality developments in Orlando. He later served as senior project manager on the American Dream megamall in New Jersey and held several leadership roles at PCL's U.S. head office in Denver before his current role in Phoenix.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE, LLC [2 Press Releases]

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE, LLC MOUNT SINAI MEDICAL CENTER REACHES TOPPING OUT MILESTONE FOR NEW WESTCHESTER EMERGENCY AND SPECIALTY CARE FACILITY

Construction crews have placed the final beam atop Mount Sinai Medical Center's new Westchester Freestanding Emergency Department, marking a major milestone in expanding access to high-quality, around-the-clock care for residents in western Miami-Dade County. The new facility will bring Mount Sinai's trusted emergency and specialty care closer to home for thousands of area families, helping reduce travel times and meet growing healthcare needs.

Located in Westchester, the four-story, 112,000 square-foot facility-designed by HuntonBrady Architects-will feature 26 exam bays, a Baker Act receiving unit, and advanced imaging services such as MRI, CT, and ultrasound. The project also features a detached two-story central energy plant and medical gas building, demolition of two existing structures and six acres of site improvements. Construction is expected to be complete late 2026.

"This milestone is a tribute to the skill and dedication of every crew member who's helped bring this facility to life," said Mike Peters, vice president and division manager at Brasfield & Gorrie. "Partnering with Mount Sinai Medical Center and HuntonBrady Architects on this important project highlights what can be achieved through teamwork and craftsmanship. Our crews take great pride in knowing their work will help make emergency and specialty care more accessible for families across Miami-Dade County."

Source: Company Website

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE TOPS OUT TRUELINE ENTERTAINMENT VENUE IN GREENVILLE

The Trueline Greenville project in South Carolina has reached its full height with over 270 tons of steel, marking a major construction milestone for the dynamic live entertainment and events venue opening in 2026. Trueline and Brasfield & Gorrie, the project's general contractor, celebrated the occasion with a topping out ceremony recognizing the craft workers, community partners, visionaries, and investors helping bring the project to life.

"Topping out marks an important milestone in any project, and this one stands out because of what it represents for Greenville," said Brasfield & Gorrie Vice President and Division Manager Ben Barfield. "Trueline will add a dynamic new chapter to Greenville's arts and entertainment scene, a place where people can connect through music, creativity, and community. We're proud to help bring this vision to life alongside our partners and talented craft professionals."

About Trueline Opening in 2026, Trueline Greenville is a dynamic live entertainment venue located in the city's vibrant West End district. Featuring a 1,700-capacity main hall, a 350-capacity room for emerging artists, a private members' club with a rooftop terrace, and a clubhouse, and courtyard. Trueline blends high-level hospitality with world-class production. Built in partnership with Brasfield & Gorrie, Trueline is designed to inspire connection through music, art, and inspiring performances, creating a must-play venue in a must-see city.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JGY-HMB3-SD84-W3R4-00000-00SWINERTON INCORPORATED - THE RECENT NEWS INCLUDING PEER NEWS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:39:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKSWINERTON INCORPORATED - THE RECENT NEWS INCLUDING PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: SWINERTON INCORPORATED

PermID4296594671Websitehttp://www.swinerton.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress5707 SOUTHWEST PARKWAY, BLDG. 1, SUITE 200 | AUSTIN, TX 78735

ACTIVITIES:

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED [3 News That Matters]

+ SWINERTON STRENGTHENS MISSION CRITICAL TEAM WITH KRISTEN BEJARANO APPOINTMENT

Swinerton Incorporated has announced the appointment of Kristen Bejarano as Director of Mission Critical. This strategic move, revealed on the 8th of April, 2026, underscores Swinerton's commitment to enhancing its capabilities in the burgeoning mission critical and data center infrastructure market.Bejarano brings nearly 25 years of extensive experience in delivering complex, high-demand projects, having made significant contributions to the mission critical construction landscape for some of the largest global technology firms. Her notable achievements include launching and scaling a national mission critical and data center construction division, and managing the strategy and procurement for a major technology company's Americas data center program.In her new role at Swinerton, Bejarano will spearhead the company's mission critical strategy, collaborating with clients to translate intricate technical requirements into actionable plans that emphasize speed-to-market, sustainability, and energy efficiency. Chris Chany, Chief Revenue Officer of Swinerton, expressed confidence in Bejarano's ability to significantly enhance the growth of the mission critical sector while also improving client experiences.Bejarano's passion for the evolving landscape of mission critical engineering, particularly in areas such as cooling technologies and renewable energy integration, positions her as a key leader in delivering innovative solutions. Her leadership is expected to enable Swinerton to address the growing demands of the market while maintaining a strong focus on sustainability and operational efficiency.Swinerton Incorporated, founded in 1888 and wholly employee-owned, comprises Swinerton Builders, Timberlab, Inc., SAK Builders, Inc., and Lindgren Development Inc. It provides a range of construction services across the United States, with a commitment to quality and innovation in every project.

+ SWINERTON STRENGTHENS NORTHEAST DIVISION WITH NEW LEADERSHIP

Swinerton Builders, an industry-leading commercial general contractor with offices nationwide, has announced a significant leadership transition in its Northeast Division. This promotion of Jay Quackenbush to vice president and division manager is part of a broader strategy to bolster the firm’s presence in the growing Northeast construction market. Following this move, which took effect on 16th March 2026, the Northeast Division has also welcomed Nicholas Schifferle as director of client relations and Robert Murphy as project executive.Jay Quackenbush, a long-time New York native, brings nearly three decades of experience overseeing complex construction projects to his new role. His deep understanding of New York City's unique building conditions and extensive industry relationships position him well to drive the division's strategic goals. Ray Haj, Swinerton's chief operational officer and East Coast regional manager, emphasized that Quackenbush's leadership will enhance the team's capacity to meet client needs, ranging from facility management to large-scale ground-up projects. Furthermore, Quackenbush is actively involved in community engagement, serving on several boards and partnering with local nonprofits.Nicholas Schifferle, who joined Swinerton in 2025, adds more than 20 years of international business development experience to the team. His background includes managing high-profile design and construction projects across Asia and Australia, as well as founding a design-build firm in San Francisco. Quackenbush noted Schifferle's ability to adapt to rapid industry changes and his people-focused approach, which has made him a valuable asset to both the team and its clients. Schifferle's longstanding involvement with CoreNet Global reflects his commitment to advancing the commercial real estate sector.Meanwhile, Robert Murphy, with his extensive portfolio of overseeing over $650 million in commercial interiors construction, is tasked with leading multiple project teams to ensure quality and client satisfaction. Quackenbush praised Murphy's operational capabilities and his ability to elevate team performance. Together with long-standing division leaders Sloane Brown and Peter Fontana, Murphy will work to grow Swinerton’s footprint in the Northeast's commercial interiors market.Since its establishment in the Northeast in 2021, Swinerton's division has delivered a variety of significant projects, including office fit-outs, historic renovations, and ground-up constructions across all five boroughs. With a robust project pipeline and a commitment to excellence, the team continues to expand its portfolio into education, residential, and public sectors. Quackenbush affirmed that the division is well-positioned for future growth, driven by a talented team dedicated to serving the communities in which they operate.

+ SWINERTON ANNOUNCES LEADERSHIP TRANSITION AS DON ADAIR RETIRES

Swinerton Incorporated has announced that Executive Vice President and Chief Revenue Officer (CRO) Don Adair will retire on January 9, 2026, after nearly four decades in the construction industry. This leadership change comes as the company prepares for continued growth and strategic development under new leadership.Adair's retirement was officially communicated to the employee-owners of Swinerton on 13th June 2025, marking the conclusion of a notable career that began in 1988 when he joined Swinerton Builders as a Project Engineer in Los Angeles. Throughout his tenure, Adair held various pivotal roles, including expanding Swinerton Builders' San Diego office and ultimately being named Executive Vice President and CRO in 2019.In his retirement announcement, Adair expressed pride in the company's culture, emphasizing the importance of building strong teams and lasting relationships. 'Watching those efforts grow into long-term success for both our teams and our partners has been one of the most rewarding parts of my journey,' he stated.Chris Chany, who has collaborated with Adair for nearly a decade, has been appointed as his successor. Chany joined Swinerton Builders in 2013 and has since become known for his ability to foster partnerships and drive strategic growth. His focus on delivering extraordinary client experiences and reliable project execution positions him as a strong candidate to continue the momentum established by Adair.In his new role, Chany emphasized a commitment to maintaining the company's core values while pursuing new opportunities for growth. 'I am honored to step into this role and continue building on Don's framework,' Chany remarked. 'Our focus will remain on building strong relationships and driving growth for our teams and clients.' With Chany at the helm, Swinerton aims to enhance its reputation as a leading player in the construction sector.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ BALFOUR BEATTY ELEVATES TRENT JOHNSON TO LEAD AVIATION OPERATIONS IN THE CAROLINAS

Balfour Beatty has promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations in the Carolinas, effective immediately. Johnson's new role will see him leading and expanding the company’s aviation sector across North and South Carolina, building on a career that spans over 19 years in the construction industry.As of 1st April 2026, Trent Johnson’s promotion is a reflection of his extensive experience, including more than 12 and a half years with Balfour Beatty. During his tenure, he has successfully contributed to the delivery of 22 aviation projects, ranging from targeted renovations to complex, multi-phase programmes. His leadership has been pivotal in enhancing Balfour Beatty’s aviation presence, notably at Raleigh-Durham International Airport (RDU) and Albert J. Ellis Airport.In his new capacity, Johnson will continue to support Balfour Beatty’s existing aviation clients while expanding the company’s footprint at additional regional and international airports throughout the Carolinas. His focus will remain on assisting project teams in navigating the complexities of active airport environments, while prioritizing safety, operational continuity, and stakeholder coordination.Jeff Sandeen, President of Balfour Beatty in the Carolinas, commented on Johnson’s promotion, stating, “Trent has built a strong reputation as a trusted leader and client partner within our local aviation operations. His ability to guide teams through the complexities of active airport environments has been a key driver of our success in the region.” Sandeen expressed confidence that Johnson will play a crucial role in shaping the next phase of growth for Balfour Beatty’s aviation portfolio.Trent Johnson holds a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and is also known for his community involvement, particularly with the Make-A-Wish Foundation. His steady leadership style and collaborative approach have helped Balfour Beatty deliver projects that align with long-term regional growth and infrastructure priorities.

+ MAINMARK UK JOINS BALFOUR BEATTY'S CP7 FRAMEWORK FOR RAIL INFRASTRUCTURE

Ground engineering specialist Mainmark UK has secured a place on the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, positioning the company to support the delivery of key rail infrastructure works across Network Rail’s latest five-year investment programme. This investment period, known as Control Period 7 (CP7), spans from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an allocation of approximately £43–45 billion across Great Britain.The CP7 programme focuses on maintaining, renewing, and modernising the rail network to deliver a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable railway. Investment priorities during CP7 include infrastructure maintenance and renewals, digital signalling and technology, safety improvements, and initiatives aimed at enhancing both freight and passenger performance across the network.As part of the CP7 Framework, contractors will engage in a variety of works aligned with Network Rail’s Strategic Business Plan. These activities will encompass low-complexity emergency callouts, planned preventative maintenance, and small-scale civil engineering schemes. Mainmark UK will be involved in geotechnical response works, bridge repairs, structural integrity projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among others.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager at Mainmark UK, expressed enthusiasm about the collaboration with Balfour Beatty, stating, “We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. On the 26th of March 2026, the urgency surrounding the Colorado River's dwindling water supply became a focal point for communities across the Western United States, as they seek alternatives to secure their water futures.The Colorado River serves as a vital resource for millions, yet it is currently grappling with severe drought conditions. Lake Powell, for instance, is at an alarming 162 feet below full capacity, holding only 26% of its total water volume. The U.S. Bureau of Reclamation warns that if current conditions persist, Lake Mead could reach record lows within two years, prompting a critical reassessment of water management strategies across the basin states.PCL Construction is at the forefront of this reassessment, advocating for investments in water reuse infrastructure as a key strategy for bolstering water security. With projected U.S. investments in water reuse expected to average $47.1 billion over the next decade, PCL is positioned to support municipalities in capturing and reusing water to mitigate the effects of ongoing drought. Although adoption rates vary significantly among the seven Colorado River basin states, PCL's initiatives exemplify how infrastructure investments can enhance resilience in water supply systems.The urgency of the situation is underscored by the imminent expiration of current water management rules in the fall of 2026. As states grapple with the looming deadline to ratify Colorado River water allocation agreements, PCL Construction's projects, such as the Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix, provide tangible examples of how communities can adapt to a drier future. This program has been crucial in ensuring the reliability of water supply for the rapidly growing Phoenix area, which has seen a 12% population increase over the last decade.Industry leaders, including PCL, recently gathered at the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference to discuss collaborative strategies for securing the river's future. The consensus was clear: a multifaceted approach is necessary to address the complexities of climate change and aging infrastructure. PCL Construction's commitment to advancing water reuse initiatives positions the company as a critical player in shaping sustainable water management practices that can withstand the challenges posed by the Colorado River's ongoing crisis.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ADVANCES $192M AI-READY DATA CENTER PROJECT

PCL Construction Services, Inc. and PCL Industrial Services, Inc., operating companies of PCL Construction, have reached structural completion on a $192 million, 22.5-megawatt data center, advancing one of the largest digital infrastructure projects currently underway in the Denver metropolitan area. On 19th March 2026, the companies celebrated this significant milestone that underscores their commitment to delivering state-of-the-art facilities designed for high-capacity digital workloads.Located on a 17-acre site southeast of Denver, the project is being delivered by PCL’s Minneapolis Buildings group in collaboration with the Denver team, combining longstanding client relationships with local market expertise. The data center is specifically designed to support enterprise-scale, compute-intensive workloads, including artificial intelligence and graphics processing unit applications, as demand for high-capacity digital infrastructure continues to grow across the region.PCL’s scope of work includes the phased construction of five steel-frame colocation data halls totaling approximately 277,000 square feet, allowing for efficient onboarding of new tenants while maintaining flexibility for future expansion. The project also features a dedicated administration building with office space for personnel and on-site operations teams. This design aims to accommodate high-density deployments, aligning performance needs with modern sustainability and energy-efficiency requirements.The project includes comprehensive electrical infrastructure to support continuous, high-performance operations, from permanent and temporary power systems to sitewide distribution, security, and communications pathways. Key work includes installing and integrating major power and cooling equipment that enables reliable, high-density data center operations. PCL Industrial Services, Inc. is self-performing the core electrical work while coordinating specialized partners for testing and life-safety systems.PCL Construction’s extensive experience in delivering mission-critical and data center facilities across the United States is critical on complex, high-density projects like this, according to Ryan Schmidt, vice president and district manager of Denver Buildings. The project teams recently marked the topping-out ceremony, celebrating the dedication, craftsmanship, and collaboration that will transition the development into interior build-out and systems installation, with construction scheduled for completion in summer 2027.
