+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION UNVEILS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ENHANCE MANUFACTURING CAPABILITIES

JE Dunn Construction has launched its new off-site manufacturing initiative, Form Off-Site Solutions(TradeMark), designed to specialize in Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. This strategic move comes as the company seeks to capitalize on its century-long legacy in Kansas City and beyond. The introduction of Form Off-Site Solutions is positioned as a response to the growing demand for advanced manufacturing capabilities, especially in a rapidly evolving construction landscape. The launch date of this initiative is 6th April 2026.Led by Off-Site Manufacturing Director Nick Effenheim, Form Off-Site Solutions brings together skilled craftspeople, advanced automation, and innovative strategies to design and produce specialized components for a diverse array of stakeholders, including owners, general contractors, trade partners, and original equipment manufacturers. The offerings are categorized into four distinct product segments: metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies, ensuring that various market needs are met with tailored solutions.The end-to-end service model of Form Off-Site Solutions addresses the multifaceted challenges inherent in construction projects. By providing capabilities that range from estimating and design to manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, JE Dunn Construction aims to deliver improved installation efficiency, enhanced project safety and quality, and maximized cost certainty. This comprehensive approach is intended to alleviate labor shortages and accelerate project timelines, critical factors in today's construction environment.Effenheim emphasizes the collaborative nature of Form's operations, stating, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and focus on getting their ideas, concepts, and design into a manufactured environment is something that sets us apart." This commitment to collaboration and innovation is echoed by JE Dunn President and CEO Gordon Lansford, who notes that the development of Form Off-Site Solutions represents a significant advancement in the company's ongoing quest to drive innovation for its clients.With production facilities located in Kansas City and Nashville, Form Off-Site Solutions is poised to serve clients on a national scale. The company's focus on innovation and strategic thinking is a cornerstone of its operations, aimed at enhancing project safety and quality while also addressing labor shortages in the industry. As JE Dunn Construction continues to push the boundaries of off-site manufacturing, the launch of Form Off-Site Solutions marks a pivotal step in its mission to deliver projects with greater efficiency and certainty.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION CELEBRATES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE FOR WORKFORCE CENTER

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — Construction has reached a key milestone on the Northland Workforce Development Center, as JE Dunn Construction and project partners recently celebrated the topping out of the new facility. The event, held on 19th March 2026, marked the placement of the final structural steel beam for the project, signaling significant progress on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story building designed to expand access to career and technical education across the Northland region.Prior to the beam’s installation, attendees participated in a traditional beam-signing ceremony, adding their names and messages to a steel beam bearing project and partner logos. This moment underscored the collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner school districts, project stakeholders, and the broader community in advancing a shared workforce development vision. Brad Hokanson, Project Executive at JE Dunn Construction, emphasized the importance of this collaboration, stating, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The Northland Workforce Development Center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services. Once complete, the facility will expand the capacity of Northland Career Center and its partners, serving both high school students and adult learners seeking industry-aligned training and credentials. Planned program spaces include flexible labs and classrooms, hands-on training environments, and technology-rich areas that reflect real-world workplace conditions.Located in the Northland region of Kansas City, the project is progressing on schedule, with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated following the structural milestone. Brian Noller, Executive Director of Business Services and NWDC Campaign Lead at Platte County School District, highlighted the collaborative efforts, stating, 'With support from the State of Missouri, local municipalities, businesses and industries, individual investors, and community foundations, we are making a long-term investment in the success of our students and the future of our region.'JE Dunn is serving as construction manager for the project, working in partnership with the school district and design firm Clark & Enersen. The project team has emphasized safety, schedule adherence, and coordination throughout construction, while maintaining communication with stakeholders and minimizing disruption to nearby facilities. The Northland Workforce Development Center represents a regional investment in workforce readiness, aligning educational infrastructure with evolving industry needs and supporting long-term economic development in the Kansas City area.

+ CHRIS CHELETTE REJOINS JE DUNN TO BOLSTER HEALTHCARE SECTOR

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025.During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region.In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction.Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively.As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS [3 News That Matters]

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS CELEBRATES TOPPING OUT OF $3.7B CALIFORNIA TOWER

McCarthy Holdings has reached a significant construction milestone with the topping out of the $3.7 billion California Tower on February 6, 2026. This achievement comes a year and a half after breaking ground on the ambitious project in 2024, as reported by UC Davis Health. The California Tower is designed using a progressive design-build delivery method, marking a pivotal moment in the expansion of UC Davis' Sacramento campus.The California Tower is part of an extensive eight-year plan to double the size of the UC Davis campus, which is recognized as the largest health system capital expansion currently underway in the United States. Once completed, the 14-story hospital and five-story pavilion will house operating rooms, an imaging center, and updated facilities dedicated to pharmacy and burn care units. Notably, the tower will also introduce approximately 334 additional inpatient beds to enhance patient care capabilities.In a customary celebration, stakeholders in the project gathered to sign the beam, which was ceremonially placed to mark this important milestone. UC Davis Health anticipates the opening of the California Tower in 2030. This state-of-the-art building is the final component of UC Davis Health's Vision 2030 plan, which aims to increase the campus's total building space from 3.6 million square feet to over 7 million square feet.The California Tower is not McCarthy Holdings' only recent achievement in the healthcare sector. The company completed the Plaza West Tower at Barnes-Jewish Hospital in St. Louis in December. While healthcare construction has shown resilience, contributing to overall growth in construction planning at the end of 2025, early indicators from Dodge's first report of 2026 suggest a slowdown in healthcare project planning at the start of the year.As McCarthy Holdings continues to make strides in the healthcare construction sector, the successful topping out of the California Tower signifies an important step in fulfilling the growing demand for healthcare infrastructure. Investors and stakeholders will be closely watching how these developments unfold as the company forges ahead with its ambitious projects.

+ AUDDIA AND THRAMANN MERGE TO ESTABLISH MCCARTHY FINNEY HOLDING

Auddia Inc. has announced a definitive merger agreement with Thramann Holdings to form a new Delaware holding company named McCarthy Finney, Inc. The announcement was made on 17th February 2026, and the details of the merger were disclosed on 18th February 2026. Under this agreement, both Auddia and Thramann will become wholly owned subsidiaries of McCarthy Finney, with Auddia shareholders receiving one common share of McCarthy Finney for each share they hold in Auddia, while Thramann owners will receive a combination of special preferred stock and $3.5 million in unsecured notes.Post-merger, Auddia shareholders are projected to own approximately 20% of McCarthy Finney, while former Thramann stakeholders are expected to hold around 80%, contingent on Auddia maintaining a minimum cash balance of $12 million. The special preferred stock issued to Thramann will carry significant governance rights, including a liquidation preference of at least $20.5 million, alongside flexible conversion and redemption features, giving Thramann substantial influence over the newly formed entity.Both Auddia and Thramann's boards have unanimously approved the merger, with Auddia's independent special committee affirming the deal's fairness, supported by a fairness opinion from Houlihan Capital. Governance continuity is assured, with Auddia's four directors set to join the McCarthy Finney board, while Jeff Thramann and John Mahoney will continue as CEO and CFO, respectively. Additionally, Auddia's executives and directors have committed to support the deal, and Thramann will adhere to a 180-day lock-up on his shares in McCarthy Finney.In terms of valuation, Auddia estimates a base-case worth of $250 million for McCarthy Finney, based on a management-prepared discounted cash flow analysis of the combined entity's pro forma over the next decade. The merger is anticipated to close in the second quarter of 2026, pending Auddia shareholder approval, SEC effectiveness of a Form S-4 filing, and meeting Nasdaq listing requirements. The formation of McCarthy Finney positions the company as a diversified holding entity focused on artificial intelligence applications across distributed AI data centers, healthtech value-based care, and AI-driven travel services.Auddia, based in Boulder, Colorado, is recognized for its innovative audio technology that leverages a proprietary AI platform to enhance consumer engagement with AM/FM radio, podcasts, and other audio content. Its flagship product, the 'faidr' superapp, aims to modernize music consumption and promotion by offering ad-free listening, content and ad-break skipping, and integrated artist discovery.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS ADVANCES SUSTAINABILITY WITH 2024 GHG EMISSIONS REPORT

McCarthy Holdings, one of the largest employee-owned construction firms in the U.S., has released its updated 2024 Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emissions Report. The report was published on 16th February 2026, and reflects McCarthy's commitment to strengthening its sustainability efforts across its operations and subsidiaries.Developed in partnership with Gravity, a leading enterprise carbon accounting and energy management platform, the report provides a comprehensive view of McCarthy's carbon footprint. It covers Scope 1, Scope 2, and key categories of Scope 3 emissions, with Scopes 1 and 2 having undergone independent auditing and awarded limited assurance. This commitment to transparency and accountability aligns with McCarthy's mission to be the best builder in America.Ian Pope, Senior Director of Sustainability at McCarthy Holdings, emphasized the company's dedication to community building. 'As an employee-owned company, this important effort is in line with our core values and provides our teams with the practical data necessary to meet regulatory requirements,' he stated. McCarthy's sustainability strategy is anchored in three primary priorities: understanding its environmental impact, acting responsibly for its people and communities, and efficiently managing energy consumption.In addition to emissions reporting, McCarthy Holdings integrates responsible practices into its projects. This includes biodiversity initiatives, responsible fuel management, and the use of digital tools and artificial intelligence for smarter project decisions. The company is also focusing on responsible end-of-life management for solar panels through its advanced PV recycling program, partnering with Solar Energy Industries Association (SEIA)-approved recyclers to divert waste from landfills and support a circular economy.By leveraging emissions data and operational insights, McCarthy Holdings continues to demonstrate that its role as a builder of communities extends to fostering a more sustainable future. With a commitment to environmental stewardship and community well-being, McCarthy is setting benchmarks in the construction industry for responsible practices.

+ CARLY REINER CACERES JOINS CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP AS HR DIRECTOR

Carly Reiner Caceres has been appointed as the Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, bringing a wealth of experience in people operations and talent strategy. On the 29th of April 2026, Caceres officially began her new role, where she will be instrumental in supporting national campus recruiting initiatives aimed at bolstering early-career talent pipelines for one of the largest building and infrastructure companies in the United States.Prior to her appointment at Clark Construction Group, Caceres served as the Vice President of People Operations at Proper, where she was pivotal in shaping the HR strategy and operational support within a hybrid work environment. Her extensive background includes significant roles at MILLER as the Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture, where she excelled in various capacities including Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ career trajectory showcases her expertise in full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder management, and employer branding, which will be crucial as Clark Construction Group seeks to attract top talent from universities across the nation. Her previous experience managing campus recruiting strategies at Grant Thornton LLP further solidifies her capabilities in building robust internship programs and enhancing student engagement initiatives.Clark Construction Group, founded in 1906, has established a strong reputation for delivering value across major markets, including education and infrastructure. With a commitment to community strengthening and project excellence, the company is poised to benefit from Caceres' strategic insights and leadership in HR, particularly in nurturing the next generation of professionals in the construction industry.Caceres’ early career involved internships and roles with various organizations, including Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, which helped her build a diverse skill set in communications and external relations. As she embarks on this new chapter at Clark Construction Group, her multifaceted background is expected to contribute significantly to the company's growth and talent development objectives.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS SECURES MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK WITH RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework, enhancing the delivery of repair, maintenance, and refurbishment services to domestic properties across the local authority area. This announcement was made on 11th February 2026.The framework, which is set to run until 2027, encompasses a variety of multi-trade works. This will enable Renfrewshire Council to effectively manage both responsive and planned maintenance services across its housing stock, ensuring that local residents receive prompt and reliable service.Clark Contracts is already an established player in the public sector, holding several framework agreements that include Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. Additionally, the company is involved with the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and various Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks focusing on Public Buildings, New Build Housing, and more.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, expressed enthusiasm about the new appointment, saying, "Building on our strong track record of delivering more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years, we are pleased to have been appointed to Renfrewshire Council’s framework. As a Paisley-based business, we are proud to be supporting the council and delivering refurbishment and maintenance services that directly benefit local communities."The addition of this framework is expected to bolster Clark Contracts' already significant presence in the public sector, as the company continues to collaborate with various councils and institutions, including the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council, to deliver essential services.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP ADVANCES ₱4-B MIXED-USE HUB AT THE VILLAGES

CLARK FREEPORT – A ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination here, has been given the green light with the approval by the Clark Development Corp. of an amendment to PKR Corp.’s lease agreement on 13th December 2025. This ambitious project underscores Clark Construction Group's aggressive strategy to attract significant investments and fortify its reputation as Asia’s Leading Meetings and Conference Destination, a title recently bestowed by the World Travel Awards.The revised lease agreement provides PKR Corp. with a 30-month timeline to complete construction and incorporates stringent performance safeguards to ensure timely project delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, president and CEO of Clark Development Corp. (CDC), emphasized that the amendment reflects the organization's commitment to fostering investor confidence and efficient execution of projects. "With this agreement, we ensure that it is a good place for everyone to go and with our coordination, we want to be able to maintain that area [The Villages], an area of preference, where people can reside and really be part of the community," Devanadera stated.PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership with CDC, stating, "We are honored to take part in Clark’s growth and with CDC as our partner, we will deliver this development in Clark." PKR Corp. operates under the umbrella of Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for its expertise in building, civil, residential, and infrastructure projects. The company has received accolades for safety, technology, and workplace practices from various esteemed organizations including the Korea Expressway Corp. and the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy.This mixed-use development is part of CDC’s broader strategy to expedite investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors. The focus on creating integrated lifestyle hubs aligns with the increasing demand for residential options that complement commercial and recreational spaces, positioning Clark as an attractive destination for future developments.Clark Construction Group's proactive approach in securing this project represents a significant step forward in the region's economic landscape, potentially catalyzing further investments and developments. As the construction timeline unfolds, stakeholders will be keenly observing how this initiative contributes to the overall growth and vibrancy of The Villages and the Clark Freeport Zone.

value.Title: California investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind project

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH5-HD93-RY1M-H390-00000-00California investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind project2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:39:34ZLexisNexisINTERNATIONALCalifornia investigates Trump administration's deal to end an offshore wind projectJennifer McdermottThe Associated Press

California is investigating one of the Trump administration's deals to end an offshore wind project.

Golden State Wind was a floating offshore wind project proposed off California’s central coast. The California Energy Commission said Monday it issued an administrative subpoena to Golden State Wind.

The commission said it is seeking documents and information about the company's recent agreement with the Department of Interior to accept a payout in exchange for voluntarily abandoning its offshore wind lease.

“The Trump administration is recklessly spending billions of taxpayer dollars on backroom deals that would turn back the clock on innovation,” CEC Chair David Hochschild said in a statement. “Californians deserve immediate answers about the nature of this payout. Taxpayer dollars should be used to build a sustainable energy future, not to pay to make projects disappear.”

The Trump administration is spending nearly $2 billion to get energy companies to walk away from U.S. offshore wind projects. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum said companies were sold a product that was only viable when propped up by massive taxpayer subsidies when they bid for these offshore wind leases in 2022, under former President Joe Biden.

The Republican administration adopted this strategy after federal courts thwarted President Donald Trump’s efforts to stop offshore wind development through executive action. Three agreements have been announced.

Under the first deal, made public in March, French company TotalEnergies is getting $1 billion — essentially a refund of its leases for offshore wind projects off North Carolina and New York — if it invests the money in fossil fuel projects instead. In the latest deals announced last week, the administration said Golden State Wind and Bluepoint Wind agreed to end their leases in exchange for reimbursements totaling nearly $900 million, provided they invest equally in fossil fuels.

Both Golden State and Bluepoint are co-owned by Ocean Winds, a joint venture of EDP Renewables and French energy giant Engie. Bluepoint Wind was an offshore wind project in the early stages of development off the coasts of New Jersey and New York.

When asked about the subpoena Monday, Ocean Winds said it does not comment on open or potential litigation.

This investigation sets the stage for legal action from California to safeguard renewable energy, as well as the thousands of jobs and millions of dollars of investment the state was counting on, said Eddie Ahn, executive director of Brightline Defense, an environmental justice nonprofit working to advance offshore wind in California.

A letter from California Attorney General Rob Bonta’s office to Golden State Wind says the state anticipates potential litigation involving the federal government and parties to lease buyouts impacting California’s energy needs and offshore wind programs. California has invested about $100 million to support offshore wind development in order to accelerate the state's transition to clean energy and address climate change.

Democrats in Congress are investigating, too. U.S. Reps. Jared Huffman of California, the top Democrat on the House Natural Resources Committee, and Jamie Raskin, the ranking Democrat on the House Judiciary Committee, are demanding information about TotalEnergies agreement.

___

The Associated Press’ climate and environmental coverage receives financial support from multiple private foundations. AP is solely responsible for all content. Find AP’s standards for working with philanthropies, a list of supporters and funded coverage areas at AP.org.

value.Title: MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - SECTOR SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH3-PB23-RY1D-D23M-00000-00MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - SECTOR SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:39:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKMCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - SECTOR SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS

DASHBOARD: MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC.

PermID4296686586Websitehttp://www.mccarthy.com/IndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress1604 Eastport Plaza Drive, Suite 106 (Map it) Collinsville, Illinois 62234

ACTIVITIES:

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc., founded in 1864, is an employee-owned construction firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 7,000 employees, it provides preconstruction and construction services for healthcare, commercial, and renewable energy projects, generating $5.5 billion in 2023 revenue. Notable projects include the Mayo Clinic expansion. McCarthys focus on safety, quality, and technology, including BIM, ensures efficient project delivery. Serving clients like Kaiser Permanente, it competes with Skanska while fostering a collaborative culture and sustainability initiatives, maintaining a strong presence in the U.S. construction market.

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. [4 Press Releases]

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES TOPS OUT GILEAD SCIENCES' NEW TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT CENTER IN FOSTER CITY

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. (McCarthy), recently celebrated the topping out of the New Technical Development Center (NTDC) at Gilead Sciences' headquarters in Foster City, marking a major construction milestone with the completion of the building's structural frame. The work is in collaboration with design partners DGA Architects, Salas O'Brien Structural Engineers and civil designer BKF Engineer.

Project partners, field teams and leaders gathered on site to celebrate the center's topping out, marking placement of the final beam and the structure reaching full height. The centuries-old tradition honors the collective effort behind the milestone and signals that structural work is nearing completion as the project moves into its next phase.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: EXPANDING HOSPITAL PARKING STRUCTURES FOR BETTER CARE

Driving your child to the hospital is already a harrowing experience. Finding parking once you arrive should not be your biggest concern. When parking structures prioritize flexibility, enhance connectivity, and respond to evolving patient and staff needs, they make hospital visits a little less daunting.

In early 2025, as McCarthy Building Companies completed the Southwest Tower at Children's Hospital of Orange County (CHOC), in Orange, California, the construction firm also expanded the facility's parking structure. This project involved expanding the structure from 522,000 to 912,000 square feet by adding three vertical levels as well as another horizontal bay to connect the new outpatient tower and the existing main campus. However, the expansion was about more than connecting buildings. It increased the efficiency and sustainability of the overall structure by accommodating a growing number of patients and staff within this care-centric environment.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: BRIGHT FUTURE: A'JEYA ANTHONY'S PATH FROM NSBE TO MCCARTHY

Our goal to be the best builder in America requires an inclusive talent acquisition strategy that enables us to find and recruit the best talent. By expanding our reach through organizations like the National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE), we can connect with emerging leaders and create more pathways into our industry.

A'Jeya Anthony is a three-time project intern at McCarthy Building Companies. Her journey is just one example of how meeting top candidates where they are help us to connect with exceptional people who strengthen our workforce and business.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. RYAN HOUSE DEBUTS TEEN SUITES FOR KIDS LIVING LONGER WITH LIFE-LIMITING CONDITIONS

Ryan House, a nonprofit that provides respite and end-of-life care to children with life-limiting conditions, recently capped off its 15th anniversary year by unveiling two newly renovated teen suites. The renovations allow Ryan House to better serve children who are now surviving well into adolescence and young adulthood and were made possible through generous support from McCarthy Building Companies, which selected Ryan House as a recipient of the company's Heart Hats(TradeMark) grants, and ARC Document Solutions.

Thanks to advances in diagnostics, disease management, and comprehensive care, many children with life-limiting conditions are now living longer than once expected. Ryan House is evolving alongside the children and families it serves, making meaningful changes to better support teens through environments designed specifically for this stage of life.

The renovation was spearheaded by McCarthy's Specialized Solutions Group, which focuses on complex renovations and technical builds within healthcare and other specialized environments. The donation involved the renovation of two private suites specifically designed for teenagers and features superhero-themed decor provided by ARC that fully immerses them in a comic book-inspired atmosphere. The rooms also now include fully adjustable desks to accommodate different mobility needs, allowing teens to comfortably do schoolwork or creative activities. Each suite is equipped with the latest state-of-the-art gaming system and adaptive controllers, plus customizable LED lighting that can change colors based on each teen's preferences. Additionally, bathroom updates were made to allow for enhanced comfort and accessibility.

"This renovation speaks to how pediatric care has changed over the past decade and a half," said Dr. Wendy Bernatavicius, medical director at Ryan House and division chief of primary, complex care and adolescent medicine at Phoenix Children's Hospital. "We're seeing more adolescents and young adults with complex needs living vibrant lives. Improved medical care, early intervention and whole-family support have extended the lifespan of many of these teens. That means rethinking the environments where they're cared for. These teen suites are not just rooms. They're an affirmation of identity, belonging, and respect for each young person who calls Ryan House their home away from home."

During their recent holiday break, Ryan House welcomed two familiar guests, brothers Mason and Jaxton, who are living with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, as the first teens to stay in the newly renovated suites. While they've stayed at Ryan House before, this visit marked the first time they experienced the upgraded rooms designed specifically with teens, like them, in mind.

"The gift of these teen suites is the culmination of our vision for this next chapter," said Tracy Leonard Warner, executive director of Ryan House. "This home has been serving families

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [1 Press Release]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION LABOR SHORTAGE LOOMS AS CONSTRUCTION REBOUNDS

SANTA CLARA, Calif., Dec. 9, 2025 - Just as individual market sectors are finding ways to move projects forward after a year of supply chain and cost turbulence, the ongoing labor shortage threatens to bog down construction unless owners and contractors embrace new approaches, according to the latest Market Conditions Report from DPR Construction, one of the nation's top technical builders.

A recent survey by the Associated General Contractors of America and the national Center for Construction Education and Research showed that:

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION HASLAM SPORTS GROUP, AECOM HUNT AND TURNER JV BREAK GROUND ON HUNTINGTON BANK FIELD

Construction of New State-of-the-Art Facility to Serve as Ohio's First Enclosed Stadium Revolutionary Design by HKS to Bring Football Fans Closer to the Field Than EverThe Haslam Sports Group (HSG) alongside a joint venture of AECOM Hunt and Turner Construction Company and HKS today announced the groundbreaking of the new Huntington Bank Field, the future home of the Cleveland Browns starting in the 2029 season. The groundbreaking ceremony held at the new site in Brook Park today marks the official start of construction on Northeast Ohio's largest economic development project to date, the new Huntington Bank Field enclosed stadium and surrounding district.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION WHERE SMALL CHANGES CREATE REAL IMPACT

Turner's support to the Paraplegic Foundation reflects a focus on improving everyday spaces at Sanjeevandeep Special School. The initiative has contributed to creating a more accessible, comfortable environment for children and caregivers.Our recent association with the Paraplegic Foundation came from a shared belief that everyone deserves the opportunity to live with dignity, comfort, and as much independence as possible. Through their work, the Foundation supports initiatives like Sanjeevandeep Special School, a space dedicated to children with special needs, where care, learning, and rehabilitation come together in a very real, hands-on way.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: CONNECTING YOUNG TALENT WITH INDUSTRY LEADERS

As part of our Young Professionals Employee Resource Group initiatives, we hosted the first session of our Coffee Talks with Industry Leaders series, with the participation of Arup Turkiye Country Manager Tulay Hatirnaz, Associate Director Duygu Tokalakoglu, and Associate Aydan Basyazicioglu. Held on April 10 at the Arup Istanbul office, the session brought together our young professionals from across Turkiye. Our esteemed speakers shared their industry experience, career journeys, and leadership perspectives, while also engaging with participants through an interactive Q&A.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION BREAKS GROUND ON $190 MILLION CONTRACT FOR STATE-OF-THE-ART DRINKING WATER FACILITY

DENVER, Colo. (Sept. 25, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. celebrated the groundbreaking of a $190 million contract to build the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility - an innovative project that employs advanced treatment technology to ensure clean water to residents.

The City of Westminster entrusted the PCL team to build the project's most critical components. As the lead contractor, PCL will manage the construction of cutting-edge filtration and treatment technologies. The facility will feature advanced oxidation and filtration systems designed to improve taste and odor, while enhancing resiliency against fluctuations in incoming water quality.

Designed with long-term sustainability in mind, the project replaces aging infrastructure and will provide reliable drinking water to nearly 33,000 homes and businesses. Once operational the facility will have the capacity to treat 14.7 million gallons of water per day (MGD).

"PCL is proud to celebrate the opening of the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility with the City of Westminster. This is an essential project that secures safe, reliable drinking water for the community," said Ankur Talwar, PCL's West region district manager.

The groundbreaking ceremony brought together Westminster City Council members, build partners, local leaders, and community members to celebrate the future site.

The facility is slated to be finished in 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION AND PHOENIX CHILDREN'S: BUILDING A STRONGER FUTURE FOR PEDIATRIC CARE

PHOENIX, Ariz. (November 10, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. and Phoenix Children's are proud to celebrate a partnership dedicated to advancing pediatric health and supporting children facing critical medical challenges in Arizona.

For eight consecutive years, PCL Construction in Arizona has deepened its commitment to Phoenix Children's, increasing its annual donation to $50,000 in support of pediatric cancer care. This ongoing collaboration directly benefits children by funding medical specialists, cutting-edge technology, and family-centered care.

"Our partnership with Phoenix Children's is about more than sponsorship-it's about building hope and supporting families during their most difficult times," said Josh Yates, construction risk manager at PCL Construction. "We are honored to contribute to the hospital's mission and to help create brighter futures for children in our community."

As part of this partnership, PCL proudly sponsors Phoenix Children's annual Big Dig for Kids event. This unique experience gives pediatric patients a chance to explore construction equipment, participate in hands-on activities, and enjoy a day filled with fun and inspiration. The event not only raises vital funds for pediatric cancer care but also brings moments of joy to children and families, helping them envision a future beyond illness.

Source: Company Website

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. DAVID CAMPBELL NAMED TO AZ BIG MEDIA'S 2026 "PEOPLE TO KNOW IN COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE" LIST

PHOENIX, Arizona (November 6, 2025) - PCL Buildings Inc. is pleased to announce that David Campbell, district manager for PCL Construction's Phoenix buildings office, has been named to AZ Big Media's "2026 People and Projects to Know in Commercial Real Estate" list. This annual recognition celebrates influential leaders who are shaping the future of Arizona's commercial real estate industry.

"This recognition highlights the momentum we're building in Arizona's construction market and our commitment to contributing positively to the region's future," says Campbell. "I'm proud to represent PCL and support the growth and innovation happening across Arizona."

Campbell was named as the leader of PCL's newest commercial buildings office in Phoenix this past spring, building upon a nearly 40-year legacy of excellence in water and civil construction across the Southwest. The Phoenix Buildings office marks a strategic milestone in PCL's commitment to the region, offering construction expertise across sectors including hospitality, manufacturing, aviation, and commercial and retail development.

His appearance on AZ Big Media's list reflects Campbell's contributions to the industry over the course of his nearly 20-year construction career, having led a wide range of high-profile projects across the U.S. Campbell joined PCL in 2005, with early contributions to entertainment and hospitality developments in Orlando. He later served as senior project manager on the American Dream megamall in New Jersey and held several leadership roles at PCL's U.S. head office in Denver before his current role in Phoenix.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE, LLC [2 Press Releases]

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE, LLC MOUNT SINAI MEDICAL CENTER REACHES TOPPING OUT MILESTONE FOR NEW WESTCHESTER EMERGENCY AND SPECIALTY CARE FACILITY

Construction crews have placed the final beam atop Mount Sinai Medical Center's new Westchester Freestanding Emergency Department, marking a major milestone in expanding access to high-quality, around-the-clock care for residents in western Miami-Dade County. The new facility will bring Mount Sinai's trusted emergency and specialty care closer to home for thousands of area families, helping reduce travel times and meet growing healthcare needs.

Located in Westchester, the four-story, 112,000 square-foot facility-designed by HuntonBrady Architects-will feature 26 exam bays, a Baker Act receiving unit, and advanced imaging services such as MRI, CT, and ultrasound. The project also features a detached two-story central energy plant and medical gas building, demolition of two existing structures and six acres of site improvements. Construction is expected to be complete late 2026.

"This milestone is a tribute to the skill and dedication of every crew member who's helped bring this facility to life," said Mike Peters, vice president and division manager at Brasfield & Gorrie. "Partnering with Mount Sinai Medical Center and HuntonBrady Architects on this important project highlights what can be achieved through teamwork and craftsmanship. Our crews take great pride in knowing their work will help make emergency and specialty care more accessible for families across Miami-Dade County."

Source: Company Website

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE TOPS OUT TRUELINE ENTERTAINMENT VENUE IN GREENVILLE

The Trueline Greenville project in South Carolina has reached its full height with over 270 tons of steel, marking a major construction milestone for the dynamic live entertainment and events venue opening in 2026. Trueline and Brasfield & Gorrie, the project's general contractor, celebrated the occasion with a topping out ceremony recognizing the craft workers, community partners, visionaries, and investors helping bring the project to life.

"Topping out marks an important milestone in any project, and this one stands out because of what it represents for Greenville," said Brasfield & Gorrie Vice President and Division Manager Ben Barfield. "Trueline will add a dynamic new chapter to Greenville's arts and entertainment scene, a place where people can connect through music, creativity, and community. We're proud to help bring this vision to life alongside our partners and talented craft professionals."

About Trueline Opening in 2026, Trueline Greenville is a dynamic live entertainment venue located in the city's vibrant West End district. Featuring a 1,700-capacity main hall, a 350-capacity room for emerging artists, a private members' club with a rooftop terrace, and a clubhouse, and courtyard. Trueline blends high-level hospitality with world-class production. Built in partnership with Brasfield & Gorrie, Trueline is designed to inspire connection through music, art, and inspiring performances, creating a must-play venue in a must-see city.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: IKEA GROUP - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING THE HOME DEPOT, INC. AND PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH1-B483-RVD8-211G-00000-00IKEA GROUP - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING THE HOME DEPOT, INC. AND PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:39:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKIKEA GROUP - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING THE HOME DEPOT, INC. AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: IKEA GROUP

PermID5001428642Websitehttp://www.ikea.com/us/en/IndustryFurniture & Home Goods RetailAddressLEIDEN ZUID-HOLLAND Netherlands

ACTIVITIES:

IKEA Group, founded in 1943 by Ingvar Kamprad, is a global home furnishings retailer headquartered in Delft, Netherlands. Employing over 225,000 people across 50+ countries, it operates 460+ stores, generating 45 billion annually. Known for affordable, flat-pack furniture and Scandinavian design, IKEA serves millions with products for home living. In 2024, it expanded e-commerce and urban micro-stores to meet digital shopping trends. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, inclusion, and employee growth, with initiatives like the IKEA Foundation supporting global communities. Competing with Wayfair, IKEA stands out for its circular economy focus, aiming for 100% renewable energy by 2030. Recent initiatives include second-hand furniture programs and AI-driven design tools.

+ IKEA GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ IKEA TESTS ASSISTED SHOPPING AND NAVILENS NAVIGATION TO AID CUSTOMERS WITH DISABILITIES IN FINLAND

Situated at the Vantaa store in Finland, IKEA Group's new accessibility pilot began on 28th April 2026 and combines assisted-shopping with NaviLens navigation to help customers with disabilities bridge online planning and in-store purchases. The program centers on two offerings: a 90-minute assisted-shopping session that customers can book in advance, with a trained coworker providing tailored support throughout the shopping trip, and the NaviLens app that guides shoppers by scanning codes around the store to provide audio cues about product areas and department locations. Both services are free, reflecting IKEA's intent to remove barriers and give customers control over the level of assistance they receive. "We want as many people as possible to experience shopping at IKEA as smoothly as possible, based on their own needs," says Catharina Van Den Houwe, Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Leader for IKEA Group in Finland. IKEA frames the pilot as a learning platform to gather feedback and refine the services for potential deployment in other locations. The initiative sits within the company's broader push to create inclusive customer journeys, both online and in-store, supported by its Digital IDEA Centre of Expertise. The World Health Organization notes that about 1.3 billion people globally live with significant disability, underscoring the importance of accessibility in retail.

+ IKEA INTRODUCES STARKVIND, BLENDING AIR QUALITY TECH WITH FURNITURE

IKEA has introduced the Starkvind, a bold new product that combines air purification with furniture. The Starkvind is a smart air purifier that doubles as a side table, reflecting IKEA's aim to merge technology with everyday design. The move comes as people spend more time at home and concerns about air quality spread from recent wildfires in California. The Starkvind launch is set for 13th October 2021.It is part of the Home Smart line and is designed to integrate with the modern home. When paired with the Tradfri Gateway, users can control the purifier via the IKEA app on their smartphone, offering seamless connectivity.The Starkvind includes a three stage filtration system capable of capturing large dust and hair as well as pollen and other fine pollutants. Five adjustable fan speeds and an auto mode adjust to real time air quality, helping maintain a healthy environment.In addition, IKEA offers a standalone version that focuses on purification without the dual function, giving customers a choice based on preference or interior design.With demand rising for health oriented home products, the Starkvind stands out by combining purification technology with stylish furniture, signaling a new approach in the home appliance market.

+ IKEA CANADA HONORED AS GREENEST EMPLOYER FOR THE 18TH STRAIGHT YEAR

BURLINGTON, ON – IKEA Canada has been recognized as one of Canada’s Greenest Employers for the 18th consecutive year, underscoring its long-standing commitment to sustainability and its 7,000-plus co-workers. The annual award from Mediacorp Canada Inc. honors organizations that foster environmental stewardship across operations, from retail and supply chain to offices, distribution centers and fulfillment networks.Sustainability is not a side project for IKEA Canada, the recognition notes; it is embedded in the business model. Peter Jones, Head of Sustainability, explains that each unit has its own targets, and progress is tracked with clear accountability.He states, "Sustainability isn't something we do on the side, it's integrated throughout the business." He adds, "When you measure, you learn. And when you learn, you improve." IKEA Canada's initiatives span energy-efficient building upgrades, waste-reduction programs, and circular services to extend the life of home furnishings, with each location crafting its own action plan aligned with the broader Sustainability strategy.As the organization celebrates the continued Greenest Employers acknowledgment, Jones attributes it to the dedication and enthusiasm of its co-workers. "This recognition belongs to our co-workers," he said, noting their daily efforts are instrumental in maintaining IKEA Canada's leadership in sustainability. The date of this announcement is 21st April 2026.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ THE HOME DEPOT [3 News That Matters]

+ HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) ROLLS OUT GEMINI POWERED PHONE AGENTS ACROSS STORES

As of 28th April 2026, Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) is implementing AI-powered phone agents built on Google Cloud's Gemini Enterprise for Customer Experience platform to streamline store phone interactions and let customers describe their needs in their own words when calling a storeThe new system is designed to instantly understand inquiries while keeping an open path to human assistance. This approach aims to relieve pressure from call centers and enable associates to spend more time helping customers who are in the storeBroggi notes that high call volumes historically pulled associates away from assisting in-store shoppers. The Gemini solution was chosen for its ability to understand natural language and intent, and the AI models are designed to integrate smoothly with Home Depot’s existing systems for a cohesive experience. In many cases, the system can interpret intent in ten seconds and resolve the issue in a single interactionPilot programs reported higher associate job satisfaction as staff could focus more on in-person support. Home Depot plans to extend the technology to all U.S. stores by the end of 2026, with further refinements driven by customer feedback

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE: HD) SETS PLANS FOR $13 MILLION SAN MARCOS STORE, EXPANDING TEXAS FOOTPRINT

The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE: HD) Posts its debut in San Marcos with a $13 million store project confirmed on 28th April 2026. This marks the retailer's first location in the growing college town, which has attracted residents and retail growth in recent years. Plans filed with the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR) show a footprint of 135,918 square feet, comprising a 107,797-square-foot main store and a 28,120-square-foot garden center, with construction slated to begin in September 2026 and to be completed in August 2027. The site is located near a third H-E-B grocery store under construction, expected to open in October 2026. San Marcos, positioned on the I-35 corridor and home to Texas State University, has seen substantial growth in housing, retail and infrastructure. The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE: HD) reported a 3.2% rise in fiscal year 2025 and operates about 2,359 locations across the United States

+ THE HOME DEPOT, INC. (NYSE:HD) TEAMS WITH GOOGLE ON GEMINI ENTERPRISE AI TO ELEVATE CUSTOMER EXPERIENCE

At Google Cloud Next 2026 in Las Vegas on 23rd April 2026, AutoZone, The Home Depot, Macy's and Ulta Beauty outlined plans to deploy agentic AI services from Google to modernize retail operations and customer interactions. The joint push underscores a broader move toward AI-powered customer engagement across physical and digital channels.AutoZone, Inc. (NYSE:AZO) completed a full-stack migration to Google Cloud, aiming to retire legacy data centers within three years. The upgrade is expected to improve performance and flexibility, with Google AI Cloud supporting enhanced system monitoring and automation of high-volume tasks to lift productivity.The Home Depot, Inc. (NYSE:HD) is set to deploy AI-powered phone agents built on Google Cloud Gemini Enterprise for Customer Experience. The new agents respond in natural language, reducing time spent navigating traditional phone menus. Early pilot results indicate inquiries are understood in under ten seconds, and rollout across all U.S. stores is planned for the coming year.Macy's, Inc. (NYSE:M) introduced a Google Gemini-based shopping assistant named Ask Macy's on its site and app, offering image-driven virtual try-ons and personalized size recommendations. Chad Westfall, Senior VP of Technology Product Development, said the tool aims to provide guided and confident purchasing options for online shoppers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA [3 News That Matters]

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE:WSM) ELEVATES AUJSHA TAYLOR TO PRESIDENT OF REJUVENATION

On Apr. 14, 2026, Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE:WSM) promotes Aujsha Taylor to president of Rejuvenation. Taylor joined the company in 2003 as a district manager for Pottery Barn and advanced through the Rejuvenation banner, most recently serving as senior vice president since 2020.In the new post, Taylor will oversee merchandising strategies, product innovation, and growth initiatives aimed at strengthening Rejuvenation's role within the Williams-Sonoma portfolio.CEO Laura Alber highlighted Taylor's leadership as instrumental in positioning Rejuvenation for potential billion-dollar growth, citing her track record, operational discipline, and design focus.Rejuvenation has expanded its footprint with new stores in Cary, North Carolina, and Salt Lake City, Utah, and has pursued collaborations, including a lighting collection with Roll & Hill.Williams-Sonoma has also renewed its leadership team with Vicki McWilliams named president of stores and customer care and Abby Teisch named chief marketing officer.The company maintains an outlook for 2026 net revenue growth of 2.7% to 6.7%.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE: WSM) TARGET RAISED TO $200 WHILE HOLD RATING MAINTAINED BY EVERCORE

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE: WSM) is maintained at Hold by Evercore as the firm raises its price target to $200 from $195 in a 14th April 2026 update. Analyst Oliver Wintermantel, who covers Williams-Sonoma at Evercore, has posted a 66.7% success rate and a 23.9% average return over the past year, according to TipRanks. TipRanks indicates the target adjustment reflects a deeper understanding of market dynamics and Williams-Sonoma’s operational strategies. The raised target to $200 signals confidence in the retailer's resilience amid fluctuating consumer demand in the home goods sector. Investors should note the hold rating suggests a cautious stance given ongoing economic uncertainties; seasonal trends, inventory management and supply-chain efficiency will influence whether Williams-Sonoma meets the $200 target in coming quarters. Evercore's analysis implies that while aggressive buying is not advised, the higher target provides a point of reference for monitoring Williams-Sonoma’s trajectory in a competitive market.

+ WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. (NYSE: WSM) GOLDMAN SACHS UPGRADES TO BUY ON OPTIMISTIC OUTLOOK

Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE: WSM) marks an upgrade on 13th April 2026 as Goldman Sachs moves its rating from Neutral to Buy, citing a positive outlook for the home goods retailer. Goldman Sachs sets a one-year price target for Williams-Sonoma at $208.74 per share, implying a potential upside of 7.91% from the latest close of $193.43. The firm lists price forecasts ranging from a low of $139.60 to a high of $241.50, illustrating a broad spectrum of investor expectations.The investment bank projects Williams-Sonoma to generate about $8.29 billion in annual revenue, representing a 6.20% increase from prior estimates. Non-GAAP earnings per share are forecast to be $13.00, down 33.82% from earlier projections.The upgrade comes amid a broader reassessment of the home goods sector as consumer spending patterns shift. Williams-Sonoma, a key player in home furnishings, may benefit from continued investments in home improvement and decor, even as margins face pressure.Investors will be closely watching how the company executes its growth plan and whether the earnings trajectory aligns with Goldman Sachs’ constructive view.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ASHLEY FURNITURE INDUSTRIES INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ AMAZON BUYS SPANAWAY FACILITY FROM ASHLEY FURNITURE TO BOLSTER PACIFIC NORTHWEST LOGISTICS

Amazon has acquired Ashley Furniture Industries' Spanaway distribution complex, a 1.1 million square-foot facility and adjacent parcels, for more than $200 million, according to a report filed on the 27th of April 2026.The purchase underscores Amazon's strategy to tighten its supply chain and expand warehousing capacity as e-commerce growth continues, with the Spanaway site positioned to support delivery operations in Washington and beyond.Ashley Furniture Industries sells the property as part of a strategic realignment, signaling a possible shift in its distribution strategy.The deal reflects the competitive demand for industrial space in the Pacific Northwest and suggests Amazon's continued confidence in the logistics sector amid rising online shopping.

+ ASHLEY AI ADVANTAGE: FRONTLINEIQ AND ASHLEY FURNITURE LAUNCH AI-DRIVEN SHOWROOM TRAINING

At the High Point Spring Market, FrontlineIQ(TradeMark) unveiled the Ashley AI Advantage, developed with Ashley Furniture Industries, debuting on the 27th of April, 2026, signaling a new category of performance enablement for in-person sales teams.Designed for the home furnishings sector, the platform delivers interactive AI role-play and real-time conversation coaching to frontline associates, enabling practice with real customers and instant, actionable feedback based on proven methodologies.It is offered free of charge to Ashley Wholesale customers and Ashley licensees, expanding AI coaching across the Ashley network. Ben Rodier, CEO of FrontlineIQ, described the initiative as transformative, saying, "This is bigger than coaching—it’s about unlocking the full potential of every frontline sales associate. Partnering with Ashley to bring this to market is a defining moment—not just for our companies, but for retail as a whole."The Ashley AI Advantage is a turnkey entry point built on FrontlineIQ’s platform, pre-configured for home furnishings and available at no cost to Ashley partners. It includes interactive role-play simulations, immediate feedback aligned to effective selling techniques, and a guided onboarding with train-the-trainer support. Retailers can opt for fully customized deployments that align with their training processes and playbooks.John Mask, Executive Sales & Marketing at Ashley Furniture Industries, highlighted the potential to improve sales outcomes, noting that showroom-floor selling relies on confidence and consistency. "With the Ashley AI Advantage, we’re giving partners a new capability—the ability for every associate to practice, improve, and refine their approach every day. This will translate into stronger customer experiences and better results." The Ashley AI Advantage is available now, with more information at www.frontlineiq.ai/ashley.

+ ORNATE HOME STRENGTHENS STRATEGIC PARTNERSHIP WITH ASHLEY FURNITURE AFTER ADVANCE FACILITY VISIT

SANTA ANA, CA – Ornate Home's founder Mehmet Uncuoglu completed an extensive tour of Ashley Furniture Industries’ manufacturing and distribution facility in Advance, North Carolina, on the 21st of April, 2026, a move to deepen collaboration with one of the world’s largest furniture makers. The visit provided Uncuoglu with firsthand insights into large-scale production and distribution that will enhance service delivery at Ornate Home's Santa Ana and Charlotte showrooms. The tour covered production floors, logistics operations, quality control processes, and supply chain coordination at Ashley’s Advance hub, a major U.S. center. Ashley Furniture Industries, which employs more than 35,000 people worldwide and operates over 30 million square feet of space globally, organized the visit with a focus on observational learning and relationship building between the two companies. During the tour, Uncuoglu saw processes from raw material handling through final product distribution, noting an integrated approach that blends traditional craftsmanship with modern manufacturing technology and logistics systems serving retailers across North America. 'The Ashley team’s hospitality and transparency during our visit was remarkable,' Uncuoglu said. 'Seeing their commitment to quality control and efficiency reinforces why Ashley furniture and mattress products remain cornerstone offerings in our showrooms.' The visit aligns with Ornate Home's strategic expansion plans, particularly as the Charlotte location leverages its regional connection to Ashley's North Carolina operations to enable more responsive inventory management and faster delivery times. Ornate Home operates as an Ashley furniture store, serving customers through Santa Ana and Charlotte locations. Founded in 2021, the company provides same-day white glove delivery throughout Orange County and ships nationwide from company-owned warehouses.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES [3 News That Matters]

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) SETS PORT ST. LUCIE STORE LAUNCH AS FIRST OF FIVE NEW OPENINGS IN 2026

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) Sets Port St. Lucie Store Launch As First Of Five New Openings In 2026Lowe's is set to open the first of five stores planned for 2026, with a Port St. Lucie, Florida location scheduled for April 24, 2026. The Port St. Lucie store will encompass approximately 94,000 square feet of retail space, alongside a 30,000-square-foot garden centre, and will employ more than 100 associates. This location will be Lowe's second in Port St. Lucie and will feature redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms aimed at blending style, affordability and innovation. The upgraded showrooms are standard for all new stores and will be introduced to a limited number of existing Lowe’s stores in 2026. New stores will also include self-service paint kiosks and buy online, pick up in-store zones.In addition to Port St. Lucie, four more openings are planned in Kaufman, W. Katy, Willis, Texas, and Walton, Kentucky later this year. The expansion aligns with Lowe's broader effort to meet rising demand for home improvement products as housing markets rebound. Redman said Port St. Lucie is growing rapidly and the new store is built to deliver value, convenience and the service the community expects from Lowe's. Investors will monitor how these locations contribute to overall sales growth and customer engagement in a competitive retail landscape.

+ LOWE'S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) PLANS FLORIDA OPENINGS AND TEXAS/KENTUCKY EXPANSIONS

Lowe's Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) discloses a multi-state expansion, including a Florida location to open in early June 2026 and four additional sites in Texas and Kentucky expected to begin operations later this year. The Port St. Lucie store will cover about 94,000 square feet with a 30,000-square-foot garden center and will hire more than 100 staff.The new locations will feature redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms, self-service kiosks for quick paint orders, and dedicated areas for online purchases with in-store pickup, enhancing convenience for customers.Lowe's currently operates more than 1,700 home improvement stores nationwide, along with 530 branches and 130 distribution centers, and handles roughly 16 million transactions each week. In fiscal 2024, Lowe's reported sales above $83 billion. The MyLowe's Rewards program offers points on eligible purchases and membership tiers based on spending, supporting customer loyalty.Founded in the early 1920s and headquartered in Mooresville, North Carolina, Lowe's continues community initiatives focused on safe, affordable housing and workforce development as it expands its footprint

+ LOWE’S COMPANIES, INC. (NYSE:LOW) OPENS 133RD FLORIDA LOCATION IN PORT ST. LUCIE AS PART OF 2026 GROWTH PLAN

Apr. 21, 2026 Lowe’s Companies, Inc. (NYSE:LOW) will open a new Port St. Lucie, Florida store, the retailer’s 133rd location in the state. The 94,000-square-foot facility includes a 30,000-square-foot garden center and an early June grand opening is planned as part of Lowe’s five-store rollout this year.The Port St. Lucie site will command roughly 94,000 square feet of retail space and is expected to create over 100 jobs. Beyond Port St. Lucie, Lowe’s schedules openings in Kaufman, W. Katy, and Willis, Texas, as well as Walton, Kentucky, as part of a broader push to grow in key markets. In addition, redesigned kitchen and appliance showrooms will modernize the shopping experience at new and select existing locations in 2026. Christian Redman, Lowe’s district manager, said, "Port St. Lucie is growing rapidly, and we’re excited to support our neighbors and local businesses with everything they need for projects big and small." Lowe’s operates more than 1,700 stores across North America

+ TARGET [3 News That Matters]

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) ADVANCES FULFILLMENT PUSH WITH BROAD NEXT-DAY DELIVERY TO 60% COVERAGE AND FREE DELIVERY

As Of The 28th Of April 2026, Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) Expands Next-Day Delivery To 60% Of The U.S. Population, Reaching More Than 50 Major Metropolitan Areas, With Many Deliveries Offered FreeThe Expansion Signals A Broader Fulfillment Push To Improve Speed And Convenience For Online ShoppersThe Program Increases Eligible Items To 85% Of All Products Sold In Stores, And Lets Customers Place Orders By 6 P.M. Local Time For Next-Day Arrival In Many AreasPricing Includes A Standard Delivery Of $5.99, With Free Delivery For Orders Over $35 In Eligible MarketsThe Effort Is Part Of The New Chapter $5 Billion Strategy Led By Chief Executive Officer Michael Fiddelke, Aimed At Refreshing Stores, Launching New Collaborations, And Strengthening Local Communities After A 1.7% Net Sales Decline In 2025

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) FISCAL 2025 RESULTS HIGHLIGHT 23.7% Q4 E-COMMERCE SHARE OF MERCHANDISE

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) posts fiscal 2025 results for the period ending 31st January 2026, with the stock up 29% since the start of 2026.\n\nThe company reports e-commerce sales reaching 20.6% of total merchandise sales, a record high, up from nearly 18% during the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020. In the fourth quarter, e-commerce share climbed to 23.7% of merchandise sales.\n\nNon-merchandise sales climbed 25% in the quarter, led by membership revenue which more than doubled year over year. Target blends physical stores with digital channels as a general merchandise retailer, offering everyday essentials alongside discounted differentiated merchandise to attract customers. While the outlook remains selective, some analysts indicate opportunities in AI and other sectors may offer upside

+ TARGET CORPORATION (NYSE:TGT) KICKS OFF BULLSEYE BUILDS INITIATIVE IN DENVER AND LAS VEGAS AS PART OF 13-CITY ROLLOUT

Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) is expanding its Bullseye Builds with Community Program, a design-driven initiative to revitalize community spaces across 13 U.S. cities, backed by a $1 million investment. The rollout will begin on 21st May 2026 in Denver and Las VegasLaunched in 2024, the program has completed 25 projects to date and has positively affected more than 250,000 community members with the support of Target team volunteers. In Denver, Target will partner with Trevista at Horace Mann Elementary School to upgrade the student wellness room and create outdoor learning spaces featuring murals and new furniture. In Las Vegas, the company will partner with The Just One Project to combat food insecurity and expand shopping areas and pantry facilitiesEach Bullseye Builds project will see more than 100 Target volunteers contributing time and effort. The expansion adds 11 more cities to the program this year, bringing the total to 13: Austin, Birmingham, Columbus, Jersey City, Logan Township, Orlando, Washington, D.C., Minneapolis-St. Paul, Phoenix, St. Louis, Seattle. Executive Vice President and Chief Community and Stakeholder Engagement Officer Kiera Fernandez notes that local insights guide the work: "At Target, we believe investing in the places we call home builds strong communities and strong partnerships." Target also reports that team members logged 1 million volunteer hours in the U.S. in 2025

urn:contentItem:6JGS-W3T3-SHKP-S0P5-00000-00MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - INDUSTRY BRIEF FROM PEERS2026-05-03T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:39:34ZLexisNexisSTOCKMCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - INDUSTRY BRIEF FROM PEERS

DASHBOARD: MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC.

PermID4296686586Websitehttp://www.mccarthy.com/IndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress1604 Eastport Plaza Drive, Suite 106 (Map it) Collinsville, Illinois 62234

ACTIVITIES:

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc., founded in 1864, is an employee-owned construction firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 7,000 employees, it provides preconstruction and construction services for healthcare, commercial, and renewable energy projects, generating $5.5 billion in 2023 revenue. Notable projects include the Mayo Clinic expansion. McCarthys focus on safety, quality, and technology, including BIM, ensures efficient project delivery. Serving clients like Kaiser Permanente, it competes with Skanska while fostering a collaborative culture and sustainability initiatives, maintaining a strong presence in the U.S. construction market.

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. [4 Press Releases]

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES TOPS OUT GILEAD SCIENCES' NEW TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT CENTER IN FOSTER CITY

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. (McCarthy), recently celebrated the topping out of the New Technical Development Center (NTDC) at Gilead Sciences' headquarters in Foster City, marking a major construction milestone with the completion of the building's structural frame. The work is in collaboration with design partners DGA Architects, Salas O'Brien Structural Engineers and civil designer BKF Engineer.

Project partners, field teams and leaders gathered on site to celebrate the center's topping out, marking placement of the final beam and the structure reaching full height. The centuries-old tradition honors the collective effort behind the milestone and signals that structural work is nearing completion as the project moves into its next phase.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: EXPANDING HOSPITAL PARKING STRUCTURES FOR BETTER CARE

Driving your child to the hospital is already a harrowing experience. Finding parking once you arrive should not be your biggest concern. When parking structures prioritize flexibility, enhance connectivity, and respond to evolving patient and staff needs, they make hospital visits a little less daunting.

In early 2025, as McCarthy Building Companies completed the Southwest Tower at Children's Hospital of Orange County (CHOC), in Orange, California, the construction firm also expanded the facility's parking structure. This project involved expanding the structure from 522,000 to 912,000 square feet by adding three vertical levels as well as another horizontal bay to connect the new outpatient tower and the existing main campus. However, the expansion was about more than connecting buildings. It increased the efficiency and sustainability of the overall structure by accommodating a growing number of patients and staff within this care-centric environment.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: BRIGHT FUTURE: A'JEYA ANTHONY'S PATH FROM NSBE TO MCCARTHY

Our goal to be the best builder in America requires an inclusive talent acquisition strategy that enables us to find and recruit the best talent. By expanding our reach through organizations like the National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE), we can connect with emerging leaders and create more pathways into our industry.

A'Jeya Anthony is a three-time project intern at McCarthy Building Companies. Her journey is just one example of how meeting top candidates where they are help us to connect with exceptional people who strengthen our workforce and business.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. RYAN HOUSE DEBUTS TEEN SUITES FOR KIDS LIVING LONGER WITH LIFE-LIMITING CONDITIONS

Ryan House, a nonprofit that provides respite and end-of-life care to children with life-limiting conditions, recently capped off its 15th anniversary year by unveiling two newly renovated teen suites. The renovations allow Ryan House to better serve children who are now surviving well into adolescence and young adulthood and were made possible through generous support from McCarthy Building Companies, which selected Ryan House as a recipient of the company's Heart Hats(TradeMark) grants, and ARC Document Solutions.

Thanks to advances in diagnostics, disease management, and comprehensive care, many children with life-limiting conditions are now living longer than once expected. Ryan House is evolving alongside the children and families it serves, making meaningful changes to better support teens through environments designed specifically for this stage of life.

The renovation was spearheaded by McCarthy's Specialized Solutions Group, which focuses on complex renovations and technical builds within healthcare and other specialized environments. The donation involved the renovation of two private suites specifically designed for teenagers and features superhero-themed decor provided by ARC that fully immerses them in a comic book-inspired atmosphere. The rooms also now include fully adjustable desks to accommodate different mobility needs, allowing teens to comfortably do schoolwork or creative activities. Each suite is equipped with the latest state-of-the-art gaming system and adaptive controllers, plus customizable LED lighting that can change colors based on each teen's preferences. Additionally, bathroom updates were made to allow for enhanced comfort and accessibility.

"This renovation speaks to how pediatric care has changed over the past decade and a half," said Dr. Wendy Bernatavicius, medical director at Ryan House and division chief of primary, complex care and adolescent medicine at Phoenix Children's Hospital. "We're seeing more adolescents and young adults with complex needs living vibrant lives. Improved medical care, early intervention and whole-family support have extended the lifespan of many of these teens. That means rethinking the environments where they're cared for. These teen suites are not just rooms. They're an affirmation of identity, belonging, and respect for each young person who calls Ryan House their home away from home."

During their recent holiday break, Ryan House welcomed two familiar guests, brothers Mason and Jaxton, who are living with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, as the first teens to stay in the newly renovated suites. While they've stayed at Ryan House before, this visit marked the first time they experienced the upgraded rooms designed specifically with teens, like them, in mind.

"The gift of these teen suites is the culmination of our vision for this next chapter," said Tracy Leonard Warner, executive director of Ryan House. "This home has been serving families
